Make small balls out of these barfs.Take care not to break them while serving.Garnished up your dessert by sprinkling crushed pistaches .Serve Gendha Phali Chawli / Sweets during festivals along with other sweets.Serve Gendha Phali Chawli/Bharma Jaleebi/Pulau ki Keer/Gajar ka Haalu/Barfi after meals served with Masoor Dalia, Aloo Bhindi Sabzi , Mirchi Bainganimasala | Spicy Brinjal Curry With Garlic And Onionsand hot steaming cup of Adrak chai at night before bedtime.

user_message: Generate a South Indian Recipes recipe for Carrot and Ginger Spiced Dosa Recipe
reference_text:
**Carrot and Ginger Spiced Dosa Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
2 tablespoons Ginger - grated,Salt - to taste,Sunflower Oil - for making dosa,2 cups Carrots (Gajjar) - grated,4 cups Idli Dosa Batter

**Instructions:**
To begin making Carrot and Ginger Spiced Dosa Recipe, grate the carrots and ginger and keep it ready.Keep the Idli Dosa Batter ready as well.
Heat a teaspoon of oil in a pan, add the grated carrot and ginger, saute for a mint till it becomes a little softer.Once cool, grind it to a fine puree using a mixer grinder and pour this mixture into the bowl of dosa batter.
Season with salt and whisk the Carrot and Ginger Spiced Dosa batter well.
Now, to make the dosa, heat a skillet, brush it a little oil.
Pour a laddle full of the prepared batter and spread it to make a thin dosa.
Let it cook on the bottom for a minute.Flip over and cook the other side for another minute and take it out in a serving plate.Likewise, prepare the rest of the dosas with the remaining batter.Serve Carrot and Ginger Spiced Dosa with Sweet and Spicy Tomato Chutney Recipe and Beetroot Sambar for a complete breakfast.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Karnataka south indian breakfast recipe for Bele Dose Recipe (Lentil Dosa Recipe)
reference_text:
**Bele Dose Recipe (Lentil Dosa Recipe)**


**Ingredients:**
3 Green Chillies,1/4 cup Fresh coconut,Salt - to taste,1/4 cup Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 cup White Urad Dal (Whole),2 cup Rice,5 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves,1 cup Arhar dal (Split Toor Dal),1 Onion,1 cup Yellow Moong Dal (Split),1 sprig Curry leaves

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Bele Dose Recipe soak the lentils along with rice, fenugreek seeds and cumin seeds with water till it is fully immersed.Soak it with water for overnight.
And then grind it the next day along with the rest of the ingredient’s and some water to make a thick and running water.To make dosas, place a skillet or tawa on the heat.
When it is adequately warm, pour a ladle full of batter and spread it into concentric circles working your way outwards, till you have a dosa of your desired size and thickness.Drizzle a few drops of oil or ghee on the sides.
Cover the dosa and allow it to cook.Once the sides of the dosa starts to brown and release from the pan, gently lift the dosa from skillet and flip to cook on the other side.Serve the Bele Dose Recipe along with Tomato Onion Chutney or Mixed Vegetable Sambar Recipe to have a comforting breakfast.

user_message: Generate a South Indian Recipes recipe for Cabbage Pachadi Recipe
reference_text:
**Cabbage Pachadi Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
2 teaspoons oil, 2 cups cabbage - chopped, asafetida - a pinch, 3 green chillies - chopped, 1 tablespoon roasted chana dal, 10 curry leaves, salt - 1/2 tablespoon, as per taste Tamarind paste, 2 dry red chillies,2 tablespoons til oil, 1/2 tablespoon mustard, 1/2 cup water, 1 tablespoon white urad dal

**Instructions:**
To make the cabbage cabbage, first heat the oil in a pan.
Add urad dal and chana dal to it.
Let it cook till it turns light brown.
Now add green chillies and curry leaves.
Add cabbage and salt in it after 15 seconds.
Allow to cook for 8 to 10 minutes and then turn off the gas.
After the cabbage mixture cools down, add it to the mixer grinder with tamarind and some water.
Grind and take out in a bowl.
Now for the tempering, heat the oil in a tadka pan.
Add mustard seeds and let it cook for 10 seconds.
Add asafetida and mix.
Add this tempering to the chutney and mix.
Serve the cabbage of paneer with onion uttapam, ghee dosa or masala dosa.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian indian breakfast recipe for Ajwain Puri Recipe - Puri Flavoured With Carom Seeds
reference_text:
**Ajwain Puri Recipe - Puri Flavoured With Carom Seeds**


**Ingredients:**
Sunflower Oil,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Ajwain (Carom seeds),250 grams Whole Wheat Flour,1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi)

**Instructions:**
To begin making Ajwain puri Recipe by mixing all the ingredients includinh whole wheat flour, carom seeds, turmeric powder, salt with enough water in a bowl.Add sufficient water and knead in order to get a consistency of a stiff dough.
At the end add 2 tablespoon of oil to bind.Make equal small balls and roll them on to a board using a rolling pin.The thickness of the dough must not be too thin.Heat the oil in a Kadai with enough oil to fry the puris.After 5 to 10 minutes you can add a tiny bit of the dough into the oil to check if it is done.
If the dough rises up quickly then it is done.Now gently and carefully slide the puris into the oil.
Once it comes on top, with your ladel press the puri down so that it helps in puffing up.
Then later turn the puri and cook on both the side till you get a golden brown colour.Once done, take out on a kitchen towel to absorb the excess oil.
Serve your delicious puffed up Ajwain Puris with Amritsari Aloo or Palak Makhana and Boondi Raita to make it a complete meal.

**Ingredients:**
1 cup All Purpose Flour (Maida),Salt,Water,Ghee - to deep fry,Sunflower Oil - as required,Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves) - finely chopped or coriander leaves can be used too but I prefer methi here.,Sunflower Oil - hot

**Instructions:**

To begin making the Ajawin Pura/Caraway Seed Puris; firstly heat oil in kadai.
Add fenugreek seeds into it and roast them till they splutter.Add salt then add maida little at once and keep stirring so that no lumps form.When the mixture is well roasted take out from flame and let cool down slightly.After cooling make soft dough by adding water.Now again put on gas after kneading apply some ghee/ghee all over your hands while you roll small balls of puris which will help frying process.Take care not to break these rolled up ball when putting directly in boiling fat else those get broken easily.Fry until golden brown.Cool ajwaniya pauras completely using kitchen towel.Pour warm oil in wok/kadhai.Meanwhile pour one batch of flour-mix together in bowl.Heat enough amount of oil now drop spoonfuls of batter and start tossing immediately.Spread thin pieces carefully such that there shouldnt any gap between each other.Once bubbles appear around inner side flip both sides evenly so that even color comes.Remove excess oil before serving.Serve Ajwaniya Pures along with Dhaniya Pudina Chutney and Green Moong Dal Tadka Dals for weekday lunch box .Or serve with Gourd & Cucumber Raita | Turmeric And Coriander Yogurt Salad For Weight Loss.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Corn Stuffed Paratha Recipe
reference_text:
**Corn Stuffed Paratha Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
Water - for kneading,1 Green Chilli,1 cup Sweet corn,1/2 teaspoon Salt - for the dough,1 Onion - chopped,4 Potato (Aloo) - boiled,2 teaspoons Mango pickle - or any pickle of your choice,3 tablespoons Sunflower Oil - for rolling and frying,2 cups Whole Wheat Flour,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - a handful chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Corn Stuffed Paratha, we have to first wash the corn and keep the potatoes cooked.
You can either pressure cook the potatoes for 2 whistles or add them to saucepan with water and boil them for 15 mins/until it is cooked.Once the potatoes are cooked, peel the skin and add them to a bowl and mash them.
Once mashed, add chopped coriander leaves, sweet corn and homemade tomato pickle.
Mix well.
Add required amount of salt and mix the mixture well.
Keep aside.
The corn stuffing is ready.
Now we have to make the dough for the paratha.
In a flat bowl add wheat flour, salt and mix.
Add water little by little and knead the dough until it is smooth and homogeneously mixed.
Apply a coating of oil on top of the ball of dough and leave it to rest for 10 minutes.Next, divide the dough into medium sized balls.
Take one ball of dough and roll it out until its about 4-5 inches diameter.
 Place one tablespoon of stuffing in the center and join the ends of the paratha dough so that the stuffing in encased in the dough.
Do the same with the rest of the dough and corn mixture.
Place a tawa on medium heat and add some oil.
Place the stuffed paratha on the tawa and cook on both sides until done.
Drizzle some oil on top of the paratha when cooking on both sides.
Take off the pan and finish the rest of the rolled out parathas.
Serve Corn Stuffed Paratha Recipe with Pisarna Manga Recipe and Homemade Curd
generated_text:
**Corn Stuffed Paratha Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Sweet corn - steamed,2 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,Salt - to taste,4 cloves Garlic - grated or minced,Ghee - as required,Water - as needed,Lukewarm Water - to knead the dough,1/8 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),Sunflower Oil - for smearing on parathas,Mint Leaves (Pudina) - small bunch,300 grams Whole Wheat Flour,Chaat Masala Powder - some

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Corn Stuffing For The Poori Recipe first make sure you have all the ingredients ready.
Keep the flour in room temperature.Make a soft dough by adding warm water little at time until it comes together and forms a smooth ball.Divide into equal portions of lemon sized balls.Grease your palm well with oil and flatten each portion rolling them out thin about 5-7 inches wide and round.Dust this rolled flat breads lightly in dry flour & roll again till they become thinner.Place these flattened discs onto hot skillet; cook both sides over medium heat turning occasionally once brown spots appear.Flip when one side is cooked through.Now take off from stove and place on plate.Invert on working surface and spread a tablespoon of garam masala and chaat mix evenly spreading around ½ inch border leaving space without filling inside.Spoon half filled potatoes mixture along center fold bottom edge towards top, creating triangle pointy corners.Press gently so that stuffing doesn’t come loose.Likewise prepare rest of stuffed pooris.Cook remaining wheat pooria's using similar procedure.Steam prepared potato patties over steam cooker covered for couple minutes.Once done remove carefully transfer the pan over gas flame and roast the poories till golden brown on either side.Serve Corn Puri’s immediately while still warm garnished with fresh coriander leaves.
Serve Hot with Lauki raita or Dhaniya pudina chutney or any other dip of choice.

user_message: Generate a North Indian Recipes recipe for Kesar Shahi Tukda With Rabdi Recipe (Saffron Bread Pudding With Spiced Milk Custard)
reference_text:
**Kesar Shahi Tukda With Rabdi Recipe (Saffron Bread Pudding With Spiced Milk Custard)**


**Ingredients:**
1/3 cup Water,Saffron strands - few,1/3 cup Sugar,6 Whole Wheat Brown Bread,1/4 cup Ghee - (for shallow frying),1 teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),1 cup Rabri,Mixed nuts - Chopped (almonds

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Kesar Shahi Tukda With Rabdi Recipe, we will first prepare the sugar syrup for the Shahi Tukda and Rabri.Prepare the Rabri according to the Rabri recipe and keep aside.Heat water and sugar in pan, add the saffron strands and allow the water to come to a brisk boil.
Turn the heat to low and simmer the sugar water until you get a one string consistency.
Turn off the heat and keep the sugar syrup aside.The next step is to prepare the bread slices and roast them in ghee for the shahi tukda recipe.Cut the edges of the bread and slice them into a triangle.Heat ghee in a skillet and shallow try the bread pieces until they are crisp and golden brown in color on both sides.Once the bread slices are fried, soak each slice in the sugar syrup for about a minute and place them on a serving platter.
Pour the prepared Rabri over the bread slices and garnish with the chopped nuts and serve.You can serve the Kesar Shahi Tukda With Rabdi either chilled or warm based on the season and mood.

user_message: Generate a High Protein Vegetarian North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Hariyali Paneer Tikka Masala Gravy Recipe -No Onion No Garlic Recipe
reference_text:
**Hariyali Paneer Tikka Masala Gravy Recipe -No Onion No Garlic Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
50 grams Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,250 grams Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) - cut into an inch lengthwise,2 Tomatoes - pureed,20 grams Mint Leaves (Pudina) - finely chopped,1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),Salt - to taste,2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds,1/2 teaspoon Chaat Masala Powder,1/2 teaspoon Garam masala powder,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1 tablespoon Butter - to cook,1/4 cup Fresh cream,1 teaspoon Fennel seeds (Saunf),1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1 inch Ginger - finely chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Hariyali Paneer Tikka Masala Gravy ,coarsely grind the fennel seeds, coriander, mint, ginger and green chillies in a blender along with some water.Heat butter in a pan, add spices - cinnamon, bay leaf, and cardamon pods and then the tomato puree.
Cook until the raw smell of the tomato goes away.Add the ground green masala ingredients, and the garam masala and chaat masala, saute the masala for a few minutes on low heat.
Cover the pan and allow the hariyali masala gravy to cook through.
This will take about 4 to 5 minutes.Once done, stir in the cream and paneer.
Check the salt and spices and adjust to suit your taste.
Simmer for 3 more minutes so the paneer absorbs the flavors of the masala.Once done, turn of the heat and serve.Serve your exotic Hariyali Paneer Tikka Masala Gravy with Phulka or Garlic Naan and Boondi Raita Recipe Spiced With Black Salt

reference_text:
**Peas Pulao Recipe -  North Indian Matar Pulao**


**Ingredients:**
1 Onion - sliced,1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),Salt - to taste,2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds,1/2 cup Green peas (Matar),1 tablespoon Ghee,1 Bay leaf (tej patta),2 Cloves (Laung),1-1/2 cup Basmati rice,1 Star anise,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Peas Pulao Recipe, wash the rice and soak in water for about 20 minutes.
Heat a pressure cooker with ghee on medium flame, to this add the whole spices - bay leaf, cumin seeds, star anise, cardamoms, cloves and cinnamon stick.
Once these spices begin to sizzle, add the sliced onions, and cook them only until they begin to sweat.
This will take about a minute.
To this add the salt, green peas and drained rice.
Top the matar pulao with about 2-1/2 of water, depending on the age and quality of your rice and close the pressure cooker.
Pressure cook for about 3 whistles and turn off the flame, allow the pressure to release naturally.
Once done, open the pressure cooker give the Matar pulao a stir and fluff up the rice.Transfer the Matar Pulao to a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve Peas Pulao Recipe along with Smoked Dhaba Dal Recipe, Masala Lachha Paratha Recipe, Achari Paneer Masala Recipe or Easy Creamy Chicken Curry Recipe, Pickled Onions and finish the meal with a dessert of Rose Gulkand Phirni Recipe.

Once done garnish with raisins, pistaches if using.Garnished with dry fruits like cashews & almonds.Serve warm or cold mathri ki kheer chilled during summer months.

user_message: Generate a Gujarati Recipes recipe for Chiba Dhokli Recipe
reference_text:
**Chiba Dhokli Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 pinch Turmeric powder (Haldi),2 teaspoons Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves),2 teaspoons Sunflower Oil,1 teaspoon Lemon juice,1/2 cup Gram flour (besan),1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,1 teaspoon Green Chillies,2 teaspoons Caster Sugar,1/2 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),2 teaspoons Mustard seeds,2 teaspoons Sesame seeds (Til seeds),7 Salt - to taste,Pickle masala - Methi Achar Sambhar

**Instructions:**
To begin making Chiba Dhokli Recipe, sieve gram flour/besan first.In a saucepan, heat sufficient water till boiling.In a bowl, mix together gram flour, green chillies, kasuri methi, hing, haldi, salt, caster sugar, lemon juice and a bit of water to make a no-dropping batter of a thick consistency.Take a plate which has almost the same diameter as the saucepan with boiling water.Spread this batter into the plate with your hand and flatten using a brush.Place this plate upside down on the boiling saucepan, so that the chiba dhokli do not come in direct contact with water.After about 3-4 minutes in this set up, the dhokli will be done and steam-cooked.After it cools a bit, brush some cooking oil on the plate.Cut the steamed dhokli into even strips with a knife or back of a spoon and put on serving plate.For TemperingHeat a bit of oil in a tadka pan for tempering.Once the oil is hot, add mustard seeds and allow them to splutter.Soon after they splutter, add the sesame seeds and saute a bit and switch off.Pour this tempering on the chiba dhokli.
Sprinkle methi pickle masala on the recipe and serve immediately.Serve Chiba Dhokli Recipe along with Cinnamon Spiced Black Lemon Tea Recipe and Gujarati Patra | Alu Vadi.

To begin making Bichi Dhokla, clean lady finger nicely.
Sprinkle some salt over them so that they do not turn black.Take gram flour in a bowl.Add water little by little and make thick batter.Heat mustard/menstura on low flame; add curry leaves and green chilies.Pour this tempering mixture in it.Now put this bhodi/bachi into hot steamer plate or idiyappam maker.Meanwhile heat oil/ghee in heavy bottomed pan.Then take one round piece from baci/bihki out of steam.After half minute remove steamed dholak/dolkis using tongs.Later pour fried dholaki/sarai into this kadhai.Know roast well till golden brown.Remove when cool down completely & then grind coarsely adding very less amount of mint/coriandor.In another mixing vessel mix together besan, cumin seeds, haldi, red chili flake, turmeric powders.Sprinkling salt now.Gradually slowly add water while stirring continuously till you get lump free smooth paste.Now prepare kachumber salad by combining coriander, onions, cucumber, carrot pieces.Cool down dhodhi/ sari after frying.When ready serve garnish fresh pudin/parsley leaves and chillie-garam masala top on it.Serve these spicy sweet treats along with Masala Chas during tea time break.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Kerala Recipes lunch recipe for Kerala Special Mambazha Puliserry Recipe
reference_text:
**Kerala Special Mambazha Puliserry Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Fresh coconut - grated,1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,Salt - to taste,1 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt) - whisked,4 Mango (Ripe),1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),2 Green Chillies - sliced lengthwise,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,2 teaspoon Coconut Oil,1/4 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),2 Dry Red Chilli - cut into halves,1 sprig Curry leaves

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Kerala Special Mambazha Puliserry recipe, first heat a heavy bottomed pan with 2 cups of water.Add the mangoes, green chillies and cook covered for 10 to 15 minutes over low flame.
You can optionally squeeze out some pulp to make a gravy.When the mangoes are simmering, prepare the masala paste by grinding coconut, cumin seeds, turmeric powder, chilli powder in a mixer jar using 1/4 cup of warm water to a smooth paste.Once the mangoes are cooked, add the ground masala paste and cook the Mambazha Puliserry over low flame for 5 to 7 minutes in the same pan.Now add the whisked curd and give the Mambazha Puliserry a brisk boil and turn off the heat.
Check the salt and spices and adjust accordingly.
Heat a tadka pan with coconut oil over medium heat; add the mustard seeds, methi seeds and allow them to crackle.
Add the dry red chillies and the curry leaves and stir fry for a few seconds.Pour the tempering over the Mambazha Puliserry and transfer to a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve Kerala Special Mambazha Puliserry with hot Steamed Rice and Elai Vadam for a delicious weekday meal.
generated_text:
**Kerala Style Steamed Rice With Coconut And Raw Banana - Kachcheri Thoran**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),Salt,4 cloves Garlic,Sunflower Oil,Ginger - chopped finely,Raw banana - peeled & cut into small pieces,Curry leaves - Few sprigs,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - few bunches to garnish,Coconut - grated

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Kerala style steamed rice or kachchera pulissery first peel the skin of raw bananas.
Chop them roughly.In a kadai/wok heat oil add in garlic and saute until fragrant.Add ginger-garlic mix it well.Now pour coconut water over this mixture.
Let the cooker boil.Once done strain through a colander so that there is no excess liquid left in the pot.Let it cool down completely.Mash the contents using hand blender adding little salt if required.Heat another pressure cook vessel along with enough amount of water such as you require.Then when its boiling turn off flame immediately.Slowly start mixing the mashed vegetable-coconut paste onto hot cooked rice.Cover lid at once else they will get cold.Serve it on plate toped up with coriander and serve warm.Garnished with more sliced onions and fresh curry leaves too.The next day also serves good.This dish can be served during fasting days like Ekadashi fasts etc.,as per your choice.Kuzhalakalichathu /Kozhi Thogayalu (Fried Drumstick Stem Curry) | Tamil Nadu Delightous Side DishRecipe: A simple delicious side dish made from drumsticks which taste great both roasted and fried.You could try these methods to make Karamani Poriyal Recipe.

user_message: Generate a Kerala Recipes recipe for Chakka Erissery Recipe - Kerala Style Coconut Laced Jackfruit Sabzi
reference_text:
**Chakka Erissery Recipe - Kerala Style Coconut Laced Jackfruit Sabzi**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1/2 cup Fresh coconut - pieces,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1/2 teaspoon Black pepper powder,1/2 cup Fresh coconut - grated,1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1 cup Water,2 Dry Red Chillies,3 teaspoon Coconut Oil,300 grams Jackfruit Ripe (Kathal) - chopped coarsely,1 sprig Curry leaves,6 cloves Garlic

**Instructions:**
To begin making Chakka Erissery Recipe, let us first cut the raw jackfruit.
In order to do that we have to first, grease our hands and the knife that we are going to use, with some oil.This important tip ensures that the hands don't get sticky from the natural fruit latex/sap present in the fruit does not get onto your hands and makes the cutting process hassle-free.
Keep a bowl of water mixed with some salt, ready to put the cut raw jackfruit pieces to avoid discolouration of the fruit.Now that your hands and knife are well greased, cut the the jackfruit in half, discard the skin and start making 1 inch pieces and soak them in the salted water.Place the jackfruit in a pressure cooker with 1/4 cup of water and turmeric powder, red chilli powder, pepper powder and salt.Pressure cook for 1 whistle and turn off the heat.Allow the pressure to release naturally.
In a mixer grinder, combine the grated coconut, and cumin seeds into a fine smooth paste along with some water.
Once the pressure from the pressure cooker has released naturally, open the lid, add the coconut masala and return to flame.Cook on medium low heat for 3-4 minutes with no lid on, stirring continuously so that it does not get burnt down.
turn off the flame.Heat coconut oil in a tadka pan on low heat.
Once the oil is hot, add mustard seeds, when they splutter, add garlic, dry red chillies, curry leaves and coconut pieces and fry  for 2-3 minutes and turn off the in a few seconds.
Add this tadka to the Chakka Erissery.
Serve Chakka Erissery Recipe along with hot Steamed Rice Recipe, Kerala Style Pulissery Recipe (Mor Kuzhambu), Kerala Style Pazham Pori Recipe (Banana Fry)to make it a complete meal.If you are looking for an Indian Diabetic meal, serve this Chakka Erissery Recipe along with Masala Jowar Bhakri Recipe, Horsegram Dal Recipe and Spinach And Millet Pulav Recipe for a complete meal.

user_message: Generate an Indian recipe for Brinjal Tomato Vegetable - Brinjal / Eggplant Masala Recipe
reference_text:
**Brinjal Tomato Vegetable - Brinjal / Eggplant Masala Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),5 Tomato - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,4 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 Brinjal (Baingan / Eggplant) - cut into 1 inch thick wedges,1 Onion - finely chopped,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - small bunch,1 tablespoon Ghee - for cooking,1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder - to taste

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Baingan Tamatar Sabzi/ Baingan Masala Recipe, we will first cook the baingan (eggplant) separately.
Add in the cut baingan wedges into the oil, sprinkle some salt and stir to combine the salt in.Cover the pan and cook the baingan on low to medium heat until it is partially cooked.
When the pan is covered, it develops steam that will help the baingan to cook without any water.
Once the baingan is partially cooked, uncover the pan and roast the baingan until it is fully cooked.We open it at a stage when it is partially cooked, as we dont want the baingan to get mushy, you want it to remain firm, yet cooked.
So it is important to cook it without the lid in the second half.
Once you feel the baingan is cooked well, turn off the heat.Transfer the baingan to a bowl and keep aside and we will make the masala for the baingan tamatar sabzi.Into the same pan we cooked the baingan, heat a teaspoon of ghee or oil; add in the onion and garlic and saute until the onions soften.Add in the chopped tomatoes and saute until the tomatoes become mushy and soft.Once the tomatoes soften, add the remaining masala ingredients like turmeric powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder, salt and red chilli powder to taste.
Stir all the baingan masala ingredients with the tomato and give it a brisk stir on high heat.Add in the roasted eggplants and cover the pan, turn the heat to low and allow the baingan to simmer in the tomato masala for about 5 minutes.
Check the salt and adjust to suit your taste.After 5 minutes, turn off the heat and stir in the freshly chopped coriander leaves and transfer the Baingan Tamatar Sabzi/ Baingan Masala Recipe to a serving bowl.
Serve the Baingan Tamatar Sabzi/ Baingan Masala along with Phulka's and Carrot Salad for a quick weeknight dinner.

reference_text:
**South Indian Grilled Fish With Spicy Fusion Sauce Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
2 tablespoon Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),2 Green Chilli,1/4 cup Fresh coconut,Salt - to taste,2 teaspoon Coconut Oil - or sunflower oil,1/4 teaspoon Mustard seeds,500 grams Fish,1/2 teaspoon Tamarind Paste,5 Dry Red Chilli,2 tablespoon Red mustard seeds - paste,3 sprig Curry leaves,2 teaspoon Ginger - paste,Black Salt (Kala Namak) - A pinch,1/4 teaspoon Chana dal (Bengal Gram Dal),4 teaspoon Sunflower Oil

**Instructions:**
To begin making the South Indian Grilled Fish With Spicy Fusion Sauce recipe, make the South Indian Grilled Fish with Spicy Fusion Sauce.
First, clean the fish, make few slits on it and marinate with plain salt for 15 minutes.
Slice the fish into two halves.Take a bowl and mix together ginger paste, red mustard seed paste, tamarind paste, green chillies, red chilli paste, half coconut freshly grated, curd, salt and 2 teaspoons sunflower oil.Marinate the fish with the mixed spice, cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Heat a grilled iron skillet with 2 teaspoon sunflower oil, and cook the fish about 20 minutes or until fully cooked.Transfer to a platter.
Heat coconut oil in a frying pan and slowly add black mustard seed, 2 red chillies, lentil seeds, a pinch of black salt, curry leaves and grated coconut on high flame.
Pour this over the grilled fish.Serve South Indian Grilled Fish With Spicy Fusion Sauce as a side dish along with Jeera Rasam and Steamed Rice for a weekday meal.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian South Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Drumstick Leaves Sabzi With Crushed Peanuts Recipe
reference_text:
**Drumstick Leaves Sabzi With Crushed Peanuts Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
Salt - to taste,1/4 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 Onion - chopped,1/4 teaspoon Mustard seeds,2 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,1/4 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,2 cups Drumstick Leaves (Moringa/Murungai Keerai),1/4 cup Water,1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,2 teaspoons Roasted Peanuts (Moongphali),2 tablespoon Fresh coconut - grated

**Instructions:**
To begin making Drumstick Leaves Sabzi With Crushed Peanuts Recipe, first wash the drumstick leaves and drain the water and pat the leaves dry with a kitchen towel.
Crush the roasted peanuts with sharp knife and set aside in a bowl.
Grate some fresh coconut, chop the garlic and onion and keep all the ingredients ready.
In a heavy bottomed pan, add some oil and wait for it to heat up.
Once the oil is hot add mustard seeds and cumin seeds and let it crackle on medium heat.
Now add in the chopped onions and garlic and saute for a few minutes until the onions become soft.
Add in the drumstick leaves, chilli powder and stir the sabzi well.
Add salt for taste and sprinkle some water for the Drumstick Leaves to cook.
When the leaves have shrunk, it means that the leaves are cookedTurn off the flame and add in fresh grated coconut and stir well until combined.
Finally add the crushed peanuts as garnish and serve.
Serve Drumstick Leaves Sabzi With Crushed Peanuts Recipe with Steamed Rice and Bengali Style Cholar Dal Recipe for lunch.

Keep aside.In another kadhai add some more oil over low flame and temper with cumin seeds.Add curry leaves followed by asafetidah, green chillies and saute till fragrant.Now pour out fresh turmeric powder and mix everything together.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian lunch recipe for Gajar Methi Sabzi Recipe (Carrot Fenugreek Leaves Stir Fry)
reference_text:
**Gajar Methi Sabzi Recipe (Carrot Fenugreek Leaves Stir Fry)**


**Ingredients:**
2 Carrots (Gajjar),1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),Asafoetida (hing) - A pinch,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 Onion - finely chopped,1-1/2 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 cup Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves) - tightly packed,2 tablespoons Sunflower Oil,1 teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Gajar Methi Sabzi recipe, firstly peel, wash and chop the carrots into small pieces.Pick the methi leaves from stem and wash the leaves well.
Chop the methi leaves fine.Heat oil in a wok/kadai.
Add cumin seeds to hot oil and wait till it turns brown.
Add asafoetida, mix.Add finely chopped onions and sauté till onions turn soft and translucent.Add coriander powder, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt to taste and give it good mix.Now add chopped carrots, mix and cook until for 5 minutes on medium flame.Add1/4 cup water and continue to cook the carrots.
When carrots are half done, add finely chopped methi leaves.Cook until all vegetables are cooked, keep stirring in between.When all water evaporates and carrots turn tender, sprinkle amchur powder and 1 more teaspoon oil over the vegetables and stir well.Cook for 2 minutes on low flame, remove the pan from stove and serve hot.
Serve Gajar Methi Sabzi with Curd Rice or Phulkas and Panchmel Dal for a percent weekday lunch or dinner.

user_message: Generate a High Protein Vegetarian Chettinad south indian breakfast recipe for Chettinad Rasam Powder Recipe
reference_text:
**Chettinad Rasam Powder Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1/4 cup Arhar dal (Split Toor Dal),1/2 cup Coriander (Dhania) Seeds,1 tablespoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 tablespoon Whole Black Peppercorns,1 cup Dry Red Chillies,1 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),1 tablespoon Sesame (Gingelly) Oil

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Chettinad Rasam Powder Recipe, heat a medium size pan and dry roast the coriander seeds,fenugreek seeds and pigeon peas all separately.
Roast them until they are crisp and the dals turn pinkish brown.Once roasted, set them aside to cool.In the same pan, heat 1/2 tablespoon oil in low heat and fry chillies for about 2 to 3 minutes and set it aside to cool.Heat the another 1/2 tablespoon of oil over low heat and fry cumin seeds and peppercorns separately till fragrant and set aside to cool.Combine all the above roasted chettinad rasam powder ingredients and grind them to a coarse powder and store the Chettinad Rasam powder in an airtight container.
 If you plan to use it occasionally, then place the bottle in the freezer and it will remain fresh for a long time.

reference_text:
**Spiced Mulled Apple Juice Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 Black pepper powder,Orange peel - from 1 orange.,1/2 inch Ginger - grated,1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),2 Cloves (Laung),1 pinch Nutmeg - grated,1 liter Apple juice -

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Spiced Mulled Apple Juice recipe, bring the apple juice to a boil in a saucepan with cinnamon stick, nutmeg, cloves, black pepper powder, ginger and orange peel.Simmer for 8-10 minutes or until the spices have infused into the apple juice.Once done, strain the Spiced Mulled Apple Juice  and serve hot.Serve Spiced Mulled Apple Juice along with some  Jalapeno Potato Cheese Balls Recipe With Four Pepper Cheese Dip and Roasted Mushroom & Caramelized Onion Bruschetta for your next party.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian indian breakfast recipe for Lemon Oats Recipe
reference_text:
**Lemon Oats Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
3 Green Chillies,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - for garnish,Salt - to taste,2 tablespoon Lemon juice,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 cup Instant Oats (Oatmeal),1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,3/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 teaspoon Ginger - grated,1 tablespoon Sunflower Oil,1 tablespoon Chana dal (Bengal Gram Dal),1 Onion,1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),Roasted Peanuts (Moongphali) - for garnish,1-1/2 teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split),3/4 cup Water,1 sprig Curry leaves

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Lemon Oats, wash chana dal well and soak in enough water for about 10 minutes.Dry roast oats on low flame until nice fragrant smell from the oats is released.Heat oil in a wide pan.
Add mustard seeds and wait until it crackles.
Next, add urad dal and soaked channa dal and fry till golden in colour.Add finely chopped onions, green chilies, curry leaves, grated ginger and saute until onions turn translucent.Add turmeric powder, salt and mix everything well.
Add water and bring to boil.Simmer the flame and add roasted oats.
Mix well making sure no lumps is formed.
Cover and cook for about 3 to 4 minutes.Now add lemon juice and mix well.
Cook for another 3 minutes without covering.Garnish it with finely chopped coriander leaves, roasted peanuts and also some grated coconut.Serve Lemon Oats along with Masala Chai and a bowl of fruits for a healthy breakfast.

reference_text:
**Chole Capsicum Masala Recipe - Chole Capsicum Masala Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 green capsicum - chopped, 1 teaspoon Kasuri methi, 1 cup tomato puree, oil - to cook, 1 teaspoon cumin powder , 2 teaspoons ginger garlic paste, 2 teaspoons coriander powder, salt - as per taste, 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1 teaspoon red chili powder,1 cup Kabuli Channa - soaked boiled, 1/2 cup cashew nuts, 2 onions - finely chopped

**Instructions:**
To make chickpea capsicum, soak cashew nuts in water for 12 to 15 minutes.
Heat the oil in a skillet, cook the onion till it turns brown.
Add ginger garlic paste and cook 1 minute.
Add capsicum, and roast for 2-3 minutes.
Now add cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric and red chili powder and fry.
Keep in mind that spices should not be burnt.
Add tomato puree and boiled gram with 2 cups of water.
Let it boil for 5 minutes and cook till the spices are mixed.
Grind the soaked cashews.
Add cashew paste to the curry with salt and cook for 4-5 minutes.
Add kasuri methi and close the gas.
Serve Chole Shimlamirch Masala with Phulka, Rice and Salad.

To begin making the Chola Ke Kapas Ka Masala Recipe first pressure cook kabuli chane along with red chillies and turmeric until it is cooked well.
I used my Pressure cooker Method or you can also make this using Instant Pot method.Add some salt after cooking them till they become soft.Once done allow the pressure release naturally.
Drain any excess water from the chickpeas.In another pan add capsicums that have been de seeded and cut into pieces.
Sprinkle little bit of oil over the vegetables so they do not stick on bottom of the pan.Sauté these veggies just enough time required for them get slightly tender.Remove the roasted capsciums out once its lightly steamed.After adding about half portion of sauteed veggies back to the boiled khol ke baichan mix all together including corriander leaves and lemon juice.Serve hot garnished by green coriander leaves at room temperature.Serve Cholke Kapas ka masala Recipe either plain along with rotis/pulao/bread toast for lunch or dinner, you can even serve it during fasting days too!
ref_name: Chole Capsicum Masala Recipe - Chole Capsicum Masala Recipe
ref_instructions:
To make chickpea capsicum, soak cashew nuts in water for 12 to 15 minutes.
Heat the oil in a skillet, cook the onion till it turns brown.
Add ginger garlic paste and cook 1 minute.
Add capsicum, and roast for 2-3 minutes.
Now add cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric and red chili powder and fry.
Keep in mind that spices should not be burnt.
Add tomato puree and boiled gram with 2 cups of water.
Let it boil for 5 minutes and cook till the spices are mixed.
Grind the soaked cashews.
Add cashew paste to the curry with salt and cook for 4-5 minutes.
Add kasuri methi and close the gas.
Serve Chole Shimlamirch Masala with Phulka, Rice and Salad.

user_message: Generate a Diabetic Friendly South Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Ragi Masala Roti Recipe
reference_text:
**Ragi Masala Roti Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 Carrot (Gajjar) - grated,1/2 cup Dill leaves - finely chopped,Ghee - as required,Salt - to taste,2 cups Ragi Flour (Finger Millet/ Nagli),2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1 Onion - finely chopped,1/4 cup Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera)

**Instructions:**
To begin making Ragi Masala Roti Recipe prep all the ingredients required.In a large mixing bowl add all the ingredients, ragi flour, onions, chopped green chillies, dill leaves, coriander leaves, grated carrots and salt,and then mix everything together.Add water little at a time to form a soft Ragi Masala Roti dough.Keep kneading for five minutes till the texture of the dough changes and becomes softer.
Bring it all together and cover the Ragi Masala Roti dough and keep aside for 15 minutes.Divide the dough into equal portions.Take a Ragi Masala Roti dough ball and place it on the center of a wet muslin cloth.Using wet flatten the ball with your palms.
Continue pressing the dough until the Ragi Masala Roti has attained a round shape.
Place the Ragi Masala Roti by inverting the cloth on a heated iron skillet and cook on one side and turn over gently with a flat spatula to the other side.Drizzle ghee and cook the Ragi Masala Roti on both sides until it is lightly browned with golden spots and crisp.Serve Ragi Masala Roti Recipe with Hurali Saaru, Keerai Masiyal and Methi Raita Flavoured With Garlic for a diabetic friendly meal.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Oriya Recipes snack recipe for Utanki Recipe - Crispy Rice Crepes
reference_text:
**Utanki Recipe - Crispy Rice Crepes**


**Ingredients:**
Sunflower Oil - for deep frying,1-1/2 cups Milk,4 cups Rice flour,1-1/2 cups Sugar

**Instructions:**
To prepare Utanki (Crispy Rice Crepes Recipe) get ready with all the ingredients required.
In a big bowl, add rice flour, sugar, milk- one after the other.Mix well until the sugar melts and ensure that lumps are not formed.
Keep this aside  for 4 hours to ferment.After 4 hours, grind this mixed paste in a mixer grinder for 20-30 minutes to form a much smoother batter.
Note that the batter should be medium and not too thick or thin in consistency.
 Once string like consistency is obtained of the batter, transfer it into a deep bowl.Heat oil in a pan over a medium flame.Once the oil is hot, dip your clean hand into the batter.
Drop the batter with your hand in swift circular motions 3 or 4 times such that the batters form strings on the hot oil and a crepe like form is formed on hot oil.Fry till the color of the crepe/utanki is golden brown and then remove the crepe on to a kitchen towel, with the help of a perforated ladle, draining the excess oil back into the deep frying pan.
Roll them up with the help of a rolling pin immediately, such that they harden in that shape when cooled.Utanki (Crispy Rice Crepes Recipe) can be stored away in an airtight box upto a week and munch during tea time.

**Instructions:**

To begin making the Utangi Recipe, wash rice in lukewarm water thoroughly.
Soak it for about half an hour.Heat ghee/oil in paniyaram pan and keep aside.Mix washed & soaked raw rice alongwith turmeric and salt in a large mixing bowl.Add very little warm water at time and make sure that you don’t add too much of water since excess liquid will be released when cooked.Cover this mixture with lid on high flame till the moisture evaporates completely.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Maharashtrian Recipes dessert recipe for Kaanole Recipe (Maharashtrian Steamed Wheat Flour Sweet)
reference_text:
**Kaanole Recipe (Maharashtrian Steamed Wheat Flour Sweet)**


**Ingredients:**
1/4 cup Jaggery - grated,1-1/2 cup Whole Wheat Flour,1 tablespoon Sooji (Semolina/ Rava),Salt - a pinch,2 tablespoons Sunflower Oil

**Instructions:**
To prepare Kaanole Recipe (Maharashtrian Steamed Wheat Flour Sweet), in a bowl take grated Jaggery and add some warm water to it to dissolve the Jaggery.
Stir well.
Filter the jaggery dissolved water for impurities if any.Take wheat flour in a another bowl, add suji and a pinch of salt and oil to the flour and using this Jaggery water, knead into a soft dough.
Add more water if needed while kneading the dough.Cover the dough and keep aside for 10-15 minutes.Pinch a small portion of the dough and make a ball shape.Roll it out using a rolling pin to a circular shape by applying few drops of oil on the rolling board.Now apply oil and fold it to half , again apply some oil and then fold one more time to form a triangle or cone shape.Make all the Kaanole in similar way and keep them in a greased container and cook in a pressure cooker for 3 whistles, oe steam them in an idli steamer for 20 minutes or till done.Serve Kaanole Recipe (Maharashtrian Steamed Wheat Flour Sweet) with either green chilli pickle or along with Appe Payasa.
generated_text:
**Kaanole Recipe (Maharashtrian Steamed Wheat Flour Sweet)**


**Ingredients:**
2 tablespoons Ghee,1/4 cup Jaggery - powdered,Mix of nuts - as required to garnish (almonds & cashews),10 Raisins,Salt - pinch,Water - or milk - hot,8 Cashew nuts - broken into pieces,5 Cardamom Powder (Elaichi)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the traditional Kanole recipe we will first heat water in a saucepan till it becomes boiling.
Once its boiling add salt and jaggery powder slowly.Mix until all sugar dissolves completely.Drain from impurities if there is any by straining the mixture through muslin cloth.Cool down this mixture little bit and then mix cardamoms powder to it.Add more ghee on top of steamer lid so that no moisture evaporates while you steam the kanola.In another bowl put wheat flour and add very less amount of warm water or lukewarm milk to make soft dough.Let it rest for about two minutes.Divide the ball of flours into medium size balls.Press these small balls gently between your palms such that they become thin round discs.Heat up the idli cooker again once.
Place one disc over each hole.Spread some oil/ghee around them and cover the pan.Now place a plate made out of banana leaf which has been greased well inside.The leaves should be clean else stickiness may occur.Make sure not to grease the outer surface but just edges.Place other filled plates next to where those were placed now.Allow cookers stand itself do work of covering both sides.Once done remove carefully take off aluminium foil.Serve Kanole during evening when kids come home after school or at festivals like Ganesh Chaturthi or Diwali with Masala chai .Serve Khaja along with Gujarati Kadhi Pakora , Aloo Bhindi Sabzi | Patiala Style Mirchi Ki Subji and Phulka's for weekend lunch.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian dessert recipe for Wheat Ladoo With Jaggery Recipe
reference_text:
**Wheat Ladoo With Jaggery Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
120 Ml Water,1-1/2 cup Whole Wheat Flour,200 grams Jaggery,1 teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),1 tablespoon Ghee

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Wheat Ladoo With Jaggery recipe, dry roast whole wheat flour in a heavy bottomed pan on low flame until it is nice and fragrant.
Keep an eye while roasting so that it doesnt burn.Once the color changes slightly, remove and keep aside for cooling.
Take jaggery and water in a cooking pan kept on heat and allow jaggery to dissolve .Let it come to complete boil.
Once it starts to boil, keep the heat on medium high and continue to boil for 5 more minutes and let it thicken a bit.Don't make the syrup too thick otherwise laddu gets very hard.
Add cardamom powder, mix well and remove from heat.Allow the syrup to cool down so that you can comfortably mix it with the flour.
Pour the syrup in roasted flour and mix using hand.No need to knead it, just make sure the syrup is mixed well.
Add ghee and knead a little.
Take about 2 tablespoons each of this dough and shape up the laddus.
Store in airtight container and enjoy.
Serve Wheat Ladoo With Jaggery along with some snacks like Crispy Sweet Potato and Poha Chivda to your guests on festivals.

**Ingredients:**
1 cup Ghee,2 cups Whole wheat flour - sifted,Salt - as required (optional),Ghee - to grease the palms of your hands,Mixed nuts & dried fruits or dry coconut - few pieces,200 grams Khoya (Mawa) - grated,100 ml Water,Pinch Saffron strands,Cashew nuts - chopped finely,250 gram Jaggery - powdered,Lukewarm water - as needed,Cardamom Powder (Elaichi) - A pinch,Raisins - handful,Slivered Almonds (Badam)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Muthia Ki Kheer Ka Halwa Recipe take jaggery in small bowl add lukewarm water.
Stir well till all lumps go away.Once its dissolved completely, keep aside.Now make fine powder from mava using mixer grinder.Take a wide pan/ kadai; heat ghee on medium flame.Add whole wheat flour slowly while stirring continuously so that it doesn’t form any lump.After sometime you will see bubbles forming at edges.Lower gas and gradually pour hot water one by one into mixture.Mix everything together without stopping till dough becomes stiff.Dust some extra flour if sticking occurs.Pour oil/ghee over the top and mix again.Cover this vessel tightly and let them rest for about 5 minutes.Grease palm with little ghee.Knead gently until they come together like wet clay.Make round balls out of these logs and press slightly between fingers.Heat another skillet/wok with half portion of remaining ghee.Fry each log till brownish golden color on both sides.Remove those logs when fried and place in separate plate.Repeat frying process with leftover dough.Steam idli steamer cook prepared ladoos for about 15-20 mins.Strain excess moisture once done.In same wok fry cashews/sliced almonds/timuttils etc till crispiness.Transfer the fried masala on paper napkin towel to absorb more oil.Now, boil panch phuli jaggery solution on low-medium flame.When syrup reaches two string consistency remove off stove.
Add saffron strands and cardamom powder.Allow cool down a bit then combine khoy/mawa and pudding sugar solutions.Add milk after mixing nicely and stir vigorously on high flames.Serve warm immediately garnished with slivers/dried fruit/nuts.Serve Mitha Ladoo along with Sabudana Kheer or Chawali Dal Wale Vangiya Amti .

user_message: Generate an Indian recipe for Black Forest Cupcake Recipe
reference_text:
**Black Forest Cupcake Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Vivatta Maida,1/2 cup Milk - at room temperature,1-1/2 teaspoon Baking powder,1 teaspoon Vanilla Extract,1/2 cup Britannia Cream Cheese - (at room temperature),1/2 cup Butter - melted or vegetable oil,1 cup Icing Sugar,3/4 cup Sugar - granulated,1/4 cup Cocoa Powder,Britannia Cream Cheese - for frosting,1 Chocolate Bar - chopped,1/2 cup Sour cream,1/4 teaspoon Baking soda,2 Whole Eggs - if you want to avoid egg,12 Fresh Cherries - with stem,1-1/2 cup Pitted Cherries - chopped (fresh or frozen) plus 12 fresh cherries with stems for garnish

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Black Forest Cupcake recipe, preheat oven to 350 degree F or 180 degree C.
  Line 12 muffin cups with paper liners.
In a bowl, add all purpose flour, baking powder, baking soda and cocoa powder.
Mix everything properly.Take another big bowl, add sugar, vegetable oil, vanilla essence.
eggs or beaten yogurt, milk, mix them very well until become a creamy texture.Pour the batter into the flour mixture slowly, mix them very well until the dry and wet ingredients are well combined.Fold in chopped cherries until just combined.
Divide the batter among the prepared cups (they will be full).Bake the cupcakes for 20 to 25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Transfer to a wire rack and let cool completely.Next, we will make the frosting for the same.
Meanwhile, beat cream cheese, sour cream and confectioners’ sugar with an electric mixer or manual mixer until smooth.
Refrigerate the frosting until very cold, about 2 hours.
Spread the frosting on the cooled cupcakes, sprinkle chopped chocolate and decorate with a cherry on top.Serve Black Forest Cupcake along with Heart Shaped Sandwiches and Espresso Coffee to your guests when they visit you at home.

**Instructions:**

To begin making the black forest cake first make a double boiler.

gen_instructions:
To begin making the black forest cake first make a double boiler.
Place water in saucepan and bring it on medium heat.Melt butter or ghee into another bowl.Now place this over the boiling water without touching the sides of pan.Slowly add milk choclate & dark chocolate pieces stirring continuously until smooth.Remove off the stove.Cool down slightly till you can handle.Take out batter onto mixing bowl.Add flour little at a time whisking well after adding each batch.Make sure there is no lumps.Turn your oven preheat at 180 degrees Celsius for about ten minutes.Grease and line two round baking pans.Pour half portion of mixture in both trays.Bake them evenly using skewer tooth pick if not cooked enough.If done bake for around twenty five mins then remove once cooled.Once completely cool slice into thin rounds.In second tray pour remaining mix and refrigerate untill firm.Frost all cakes by spreading some mascarpone cheese mixture between layers.For that take heavy whipping cream whip light but stiff peaks.Heat up microwave safe glass bowl; warm it in microwave for few seconds.Then slowly fold rest frosting while heating.To assemble start pouring layer of whipped topping followed by moistened sponge piece and finish again before finishing.Serve these delicious treats during parties alongwith ice cold champagne!Serve Black Forest Cake along with Lemon Lavender Ice Cream and Fresh Fruit Tartine With Balsamic Glaze for party appetizers.

user_message: Generate an Indian recipe for Tandoori Chickpea & Cauliflower Curry Recipe (Vegan Curry)
reference_text:
**Tandoori Chickpea & Cauliflower Curry Recipe (Vegan Curry)**


**Ingredients:**
1 Cauliflower (gobi) - cut into florets,1 teaspoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder,1 or 2 Coal - pieces of coal for smoking,1 cup Kabuli Chana (White Chickpeas) - boiled,2 cloves Garlic,1 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,2 teaspoons Fennel seeds (Saunf) - roasted,Sunflower Oil - for cooking,1/2 teaspoon Chaat Masala Powder,3 sprig Mint Leaves (Pudina) - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),2 cups Homemade tomato puree

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Tandoori Chickpea & Cauliflower Curry in Tomato Curry Recipe, we will first have to get all the ingredients ready.
Have 1/2 cup of raw chickpea soaked for 8 hours and cooked until soft.You should have one cup of cooked chickpeas.
 Keep this aside.Puree the tomatoes according to the instructions (link) and keep aside.Pound the fennel seeds in a pestle and mortar until it becomes a coarse powder and keep aside.The next step is to cook the cauliflower to make the tandoori curry.In a large wok or a kadai, heat a teaspoon of oil.
Add the cauliflower florets, sprinkle some salt and stir fry the cauliflower until it is cooked through.
It should be firm, and yet cooked.This will take anywhere between 5 to 10 minutes.
Once the cauliflower is cooked, add the turmeric powder, red chili powder, garam masala powder, fennel seed powder and give it a stir.
Add the tomato puree, chickpea and stir well to combine the curry.Check the salt and spice levels and adjust to suit your taste.
Turn the heat to medium high and give the curry a brisk simmer for about 5 minutes.
While it is simmering we will prepare the coal for smoking.To begin, first take a piece of coal (about 3 inches big) and hold it over a gas flame with the help of tongs.
The coal will begin to turn red hot and grey in about 5 minutes.
This indicates that the coal is ready to be used for smoking.
Place this in a a small steel or a glass bowlMake a small well in the centre of the kadai where the cauliflower is simmering and place the cup into it.
Pour a spoon of ghee on the coal.It will begin to smoke.
Immediately cover the pan and reduce the heat to low and allow it to rest for about a minute until all the smoke as died down.
Open the lid and remove the coal cup.
The Cauliflower and Chickpea Curry will have a smoked tandoori effect into the dish.Once done, turn off the heat and transfer the Tandoori Cauliflower Chickpea Curry into a serving dish and sprinkle some mint leaves.Serve the Tandoori Chickpea & Cauliflower Curry in Tomato Curry along with hot steamed rice and a salad for a wholesome weeknight dinner.
generated_text:
**Tandoori Chicken Tikka Masala Style Vegetarian Spicy Grilled Eggplant | Kandari Gobhi Ki Sabzi **


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Kabuli Chana (White chickpeas),2 tablespoon Curd (Dahi / Yogurt) - whisked,Water - as required to soak the lentils,Salt - to taste,Curry leaves - few sprigs,Red Chilli powder - a pinch,Coriander Powder (Dhania) - or garam masala powder,1/4 teaspoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Flakes,Lemon juice - from one lemon,Ghee - or oil,400 grams Brinjal (Baingan / Baondi/Bagora) - cut into wedges,200 gram Onion - chopped small pieces,Fresh coconut - grated,Asafoetida (hing)

**Instructions:**
To begin making Tawa Pindi Ka Shalgam Sabzi we first pressure cook kaddu chane in enough water till it is soft.
Once done allow them drain all excess of water.Heat some ghee in heavy bottom pan.Add curry leaves and hing.Cut brinjals length wise into thin slices such that each piece has two parts together.Take each part on your hand apply tamarind paste evenly using fingers, sprinkle salt, red chillies powder, coriander powder and keep aside.In another bowl mix curd ,coconut and lime juice well.Now take one by one slice add half of yogurt mixture over it .Keep this marination side by side in room fan for at least 30 minutes.Prepare same way for rest of the slits.Place these coated brinjas on heated pan and fry until golden brown on both sides.Drain out any extra marinade when fried.Serve hot along with jeera rice, phulka's and pickle.

user_message: Generate a Diabetic Friendly Bihari lunch recipe for Chana Turai/Nenua Chana Sabzi Recipe (Ridge Gourd with Black Chana Recipe)
reference_text:
**Chana Turai/Nenua Chana Sabzi Recipe (Ridge Gourd with Black Chana Recipe)**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 cup Kala Chana (Brown Chickpeas) - soaked overnight,4 cloves Garlic,2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,2 Onions - sliced,500 grams Ridge Gourd (Turai/ Peerkangai) - sliced,2 Green Chilli - chopped,1 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 tablespoon Mustard oil,1 teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder),1 pinch Asafoetida (hing)

**Instructions:**
We begin making the Chana Turai/Nenua Chana Sabzi Recipe (Ridge Gourd with Black Chana Recipe) by soaking the chana overnight or for 8 hours in water.Heat a kadai with oil, splutter the mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds and hing.
Add chopped onions and fry them till it is translucent.
Add in the green chillies after few minutes and Give it a stir.Next add all the red chilli powder, turmeric powder and coriander powder and mix well.
Now at this point add the soaked channa along with the water and mix.Boil it over for few seconds till the water is all evaporated.
Then add the chopped ridge gourd and mix well.
You can also ½ cup of water and close it and cook.Once you remove the lid add the mango powder and cumin powder and mix well.
Saute till the water is all evaporated and serve hot.
Serve the Chana Turai/Nenua Chana Sabzi Recipe (Ridge Gourd with Black Chana Recipe) along with Hot Phulka or steamed Rice as your healthy Lunch.
generated_text:
**Channa Turia/ Nenua Chane Saabji | Ragi Flour Bhindi Ki Subzi - A Healthy South India Style Curry Recipe **


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Cumin powder, salt - as per taste,Salt - to taste,2 Dry Red Chillies,250 grams Ridge Gourds - finely chopped,300 gram Rajma,4 cloves Garlic - paste,Curry leaves - Few sprigs,Coconut Oil - or ghee ,1 Onion - thinly sliced,5 Green chilli - slit lengthwise,Asafoetida (hing),Mustard seeds - few tsp,500 grams Elephant yam (Suran/Senai/Ratalu)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Chole Tuvar Dal / Nagli Nu Shaak Recipe, pressure cook chola beans along with little water in a cooker till it is just cooked.
Once done mash them lightly using fork.Heat oil/ghee in a heavy bottomed pan on medium flame; add mustard seeds and allow it splutter.Add hing & curry leaves and let it crackle.Add onions saute until they turn translucent.Now add garlic, green chili, red dry chilly and stir well.Sprinkle some water and cover the lid of the kadhai.Pressure cook this mixture for about three whistles on high heat.Once cooled down release the steam naturally.Open the pot and check if there's any excess moisture left inside.Cover once again place over low-medium flame.Turn off the gas when you see that most of its absorbed all the liquid.In another Kadhi Pan, heat oil and temper it by adding cumin seeds.When the seeds start sputtering add fenugreek seeds, crushed red chillies and tur dal.Stir fry everything together so that masala gets coated evenly onto lentils.Add salt according to your palate.Lower the flames and mix gently while stirring occasionally for even roasting.Remove from stove top after around five minutes.Switch off the gas.
Add freshly grated coconut and give a gentle boil.Garnish Raghu nu shaak with coriander leaves.Serve raghu nu shaka with hot steamed rice and beetroot sambar/poorachyaku poriyal or Phulka and papad for delicious weeknight dinner.

user_message: Generate a Diabetic Friendly South Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Sundakkai and Methi Leaves Sambar Recipe - Sundkai Methi Sambar Recipe
reference_text:
**Sundakkai and Methi Leaves Sambar Recipe - Sundkai Methi Sambar Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
green coriander - little, oil - as per use, 1/4 cup fenugreek, curry leaves - little, 1/2 Tomato, 1/2 tsp Turmeric Powder, water - According to use, 1/4 teaspoon cumin seeds,1/2 cup Sundkai, 1/2 teaspoon mustard, 1/2 cup Arhar dal - boiled and mashed, 1/4 teaspoon asafoetida, salt - as per taste, 1 Tamarind, 1 tsp Coriander Powder, 2 tsp Sambar Powder, 1 dry red chili

**Instructions:**
To make the Sundakai Methi Sambar recipe, first take out the stem of the Sundakai and cut it.
Clean and wash the fenugreek and keep it separately.
Mix tamarind with water in a bowl.
Now heat oil in a pan.
Add asafetida to it and cook it for 10 seconds.
After 10 seconds, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, dry red chilies and cook till the mustard seeds crackle.
Now add the sundkai and cook for 3 to 4 minutes.
Add curry leaves and mix.
Now add fenugreek and cook till the fenugreek becomes soft.
After cooking, add tomatoes, turmeric powder, sambar powder, coriander powder, salt, mix and cook for 1 minute.
After 1 minute add tamarind water, remaining water and let it boil.
After boiling for 8 to 10 minutes, add the dal to it and mix.
Cook until the sambar becomes thick and then garnish with green coriander.
Serve Sundkai Methi Sambar Recipe along with rice, beetroot thoran and papad for dinner.

**Instructions:**

user_message: Generate a Non Vegeterian Parsi Recipes lunch recipe for Patra Ni Machi Recipe - Fish Cooked In Banana Leaf
reference_text:
**Patra Ni Machi Recipe - Fish Cooked In Banana Leaf**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Sugar,1 cup Coriander (Dhania) Leaves,8 cloves Garlic,1 cup Mint Leaves (Pudina),5 Green Chillies,1 teaspoon Lemon juice,3 Pomfret fish,Banana leaf - to wrap the fish,1/2 cup Fresh coconut - grated,1-1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 teaspoon Salt

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Patra Ni Machi Recipe, Into a blender add all the ingredients mentioned under the list ‘For the chutney’ like the coconut, coriander leaves, mint leaves, green chillies, cumin seeds, salt, sugar and lemon juice and blend into fine thick paste.
You need not add any water, make sure your paste is fine and thick.Clean the fish.
Make small slits on the fish on both sides.
Smear good amount of green chutney on both the sides of the fish.Place a steamer with little little water on heat.Now take a pieces of banana leaf and place it on steamer which is boiling.
This helps the banana leaf to become soft and easy to fold without breaking or tearing.
Place the banana leaf on steamer for about a minute on each side.
Then remove it off.Place the fish on the banana leaf now and then fold it on all four sides to make a parcel.Place the wrapped banana leaf on steamer and steam for about 10 to 14 minutes until the fish is cooked through.Once done remove the fish from the steamer, carefully unwrap the banana leaf and transfer the steamed fish onto a serving plate and serve immediately.Serve Patra Ni Machi on its own along with some Steamed Rice and Vegetable Dhansak or a perfect Parsi meal.

reference_text:
**Green Chilli Vegetable Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 tablespoon Lemon juice,1/2 tablespoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1/2 tablespoon Salt,200 grams Green Chillies,1/4 tablespoon Red Chilli powder,1 tablespoon Sunflower Oil,1/2 teaspoon Sugar,1 tablespoon Mustard seeds,1/2 tablespoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1/2 tablespoon Fennel Powder

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Green Chilli Vegetable recipe, take green chillies and cut into small pieces.Heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan and add mustard seeds.
Once the mustard seeds starts to crackle, add green chillies.Add turmeric powder, salt, chilli powder, fennel powder, coriander powder.
Mix it well and let it cook for 3 to 4 minutes.Once it is cooked, then add lime juice and sugar.
Mix well and cook for another one minute.Serve Green Chilli Vegetable along with Panchmel Dal and Hot Phulkas for a simple and delicious weekday lunch or dinner.

Drain well.Cut them into roundels or thin slices like we do for paratha filling.Pressure cook brindles alongwith salt, turmeric until done.Once cooked release pressure naturally.Strain out excess oil by pressing it between your hands.In same wok heat ghee on medium flame; add garlic pods and saute till they soften.Add curry leaf and stir fry again.Now squeeze okras so that their moisture evaporates completely.When everything is roasted nicely then you can transfer this mixture onto plate.Heat some more ghee in pan and pour over sliced green chillies.Garnish with coriander and serve hot.Serve Palak Ki Pyaz ki sabzis with Phulka's, Dal Banjasla , Tawa Parathas or even Steamed Rice .

user_message: Generate a High Protein Vegetarian Maharashtrian Recipes snack recipe for Khajur Satori Recipe (Dates Flatbread)
reference_text:
**Khajur Satori Recipe (Dates Flatbread)**


**Ingredients:**
2 tablespoon Milk Powder,1 tablespoon Ghee - or butter,1 cup Dates,2 teaspoon Poppy seeds,Salt - a pinch,2 tablespoon Dry coconut (kopra) - grated,1/2 cup Milk,1 teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),1 cup Whole Wheat Flour - + flour for dusting

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Khajur Satori recipe, remove seeds from the dates if you are not using seedless dates.
Chop them roughly and add them to a grinder.
Make a paste and keep it aside in a separate bowl.Dry roast poppy seeds and coconut in a pan separately until the raw smell goes away.
Add it to the dates paste.
Mix everything and keep it aside with a lid on it.Get a bowl and add whole wheat flour in it.
Add salt and mix it.Heat a pan and add ghee/butter in it.
Add this hot ghee over the wheat flour.
Mix everything with your hands.
Make a medium soft dough by adding little milk at a time.
Cover it with a damp cloth and keep it aside for one hour.
After one hour, knead the dough again and make equal size balls.
Get a stuffing and make the same amount of balls.Get one flour ball and roll out in a small disk using rolling pin.
Get a dates stuffing ball and place it in the centre of rolled flour ball.
Gather the edges and seal them together.Flatten this ball with your hands and roll out in a small circle using very little flour for dusting.
The 'Satori' should not be thin.Heat tava/griddle and place this rolled flat bread and brush the sides and the centre with some ghee/butter.
Cook from both the sides by flipping the other side.Serve hot or cold with ghee/butter.
You can store these for a week in a airtight container.Serve Khajur Satori as a snack along with Masala Chai and Carrots and Coriander fritters for your tea time break.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian South Indian Recipes south indian breakfast recipe for Godhumai Idli Recipe (Instant Whole Wheat Flour Idli)
reference_text:
**Godhumai Idli Recipe (Instant Whole Wheat Flour Idli)**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),1/2 teaspoon Chana dal (Bengal Gram Dal) - split,5 Cashew nuts - broken roughly,1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1 Green Chilli - finely chopped,Salt - to taste,1/2 teaspoon Enos fruit salt,1 teaspoon Sesame (Gingelly) Oil - to grease the Idli Plates,1 sprig Curry leaves,1 cup Whole Wheat Flour,1/4 teaspoon Whole Black Peppercorns - crushed,2 teaspoons Ghee,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Godhumai Idli Recipe (Instant Whole Wheat Flour Idli), first get all the ingredients ready and keep aside.Heat a pan over low heat and dry roast the wheat flour till they turn fragrant.Keep stirring continuously to roast the flour uniformly and avoid burning.
Transfer the flour to a another vessel and allow it to cool.Heat the pan again with ghee and add the ingredients given under ' seasoning' in the order given.
Allow them to roast and crackle and set it aside.Whisk together yogurt and 1 cup of water to make a buttermilk.Add buttermilk to the flour and using a hand whisk, stir to form a batter adding more water if required to make a thick batter.
Once you have a thick batter, add the tempering to the batter and mix well.Add salt ( just a little) and allow the batter to rest for 20-30 minutes.
In the meantime, grease the Idli plates and preheat the steamer.Once you are ready to steam the idlis, add 1/2 teaspoon of enos fruit salt and stir the batter.
The batter will become light and fluffy.Spoon the batter into the greased plate and steam the Idlis for 15 -20 minutes until done.Once done, turn off the heat and allow it to rest for 5 minutes.
Remove Godhumai Idli from the plates and serve.Serve these healthy Godhumai Idlis along with Tomato Onion Chutney, Thakkali Vengaya Sambar or Idli Milagai Podi for breakfast or dinner.

user_message: Generate a High Protein Vegetarian Chettinad dinner recipe for Chettinad Mint Potato Biryani Recipe
reference_text:
**Chettinad Mint Potato Biryani Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Paneer (Homemade Cottage Cheese) - cut into cubes,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 Onion - chopped,2 Potato (Aloo) - cut into cubes,Sunflower Oil - as required,2 tablespoon Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),Salt - to taste,2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds,1 teaspoon Biryani Masala Powder,1/2 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),2 teaspoons Garam masala powder,1/2 inch Ginger,Mint Leaves (Pudina) - chopped (as required),6 cloves Garlic - crushed,3 Cloves (Laung),1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 cup Basmati rice,1 Tomato,1 teaspoon Fennel seeds (Saunf),1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1 Onion,2 Green Chillies - chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Chettinad Mint Potato Biryani Recipe, wash the rice and soak it for 10 minutes.
I have used seeraga/jeeraga samba rice.
Chop the onion and tomato lengthwise.
Crush the garlic and ginger.Wash and chop potatoes into cubes.
Slice the paneer into cubes.Take one chopped onion, mint leaves and fennel seeds in a blender.
Add a little water and grind into a puree.
Keep aside.Heat oil in a pressure cooker, add the spices listed under “dry spices" including cinnamom stick, cloves, cardamom and bay leaf.
Saute for a minute.Add chopped onion and saute until translucent.
Add slit green chilli and crushed ginger garlic.Saute well until the raw smell goes.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until it turns soft and mushy.Now add the ground mint puree, curd, few mint leaves, potato and paneer cubes.
Stir and cook until the oil separates.Add coriander powder, red chilli powder, garam masala and biryani masala powder.
Mix and cook for 5 minutes.Add rice along with required water to it.
Mix and bring to boil.Close the lid and pressure cook for 3 whistles.
Once done, wait until the pressure releases naturally and open the lid.
Fluff the rice gently and serve hot.Serve Chettinad Mint Potato Biryani along with Mirchi Ka Salan and , Burani Raita or any raita of your choice.

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Pachai Masiyal Sambar Sadam Recipe , wash potatoes thoroughly.
Boil them in water until they become soft.Take out all the skin of potato pieces once it cools down completely.Cut into medium cubes.In another pan add oil on low flame.Add coriander seeds, cumin seeds & let them sizzle.Now add garlic pods one by one till brownish.Once done switch off the gas.Pressure cook mint along with some curry leaves, black peppercorns in half glass of water up to two whistles.Turn off the heat.Allow pressure cooker release naturally.Meanwhile mix curd well together with mashed boiled potatoes, seasonings including rock salt, freshly ground masala, bread crumb & keep aside.Heat ghee in Kadhai over slow flame on which you will shallow fry your biryani.Fry these dumplings nicely in hot Ghee.After frying transfer this mixture onto absorbent paper napkin so that excess moisture is absorbed.Next take fried spicy moong dal rice cooked separately in separate vessel.When everything gets ready, pour yogurt-mixed potato paste on top.Sprinkle fresh cut green corianderr and sprinkle more toasted breadcrumbs around.Serve steaming warm Chettinad style Moong Dal Payasam/Biriyani garnished with crispy onions if desired.You can also serve tomato onion raita or any other Raita of choice to go along with this delicious homemade meal.

user_message: Generate a High Protein Non Vegetarian North East India Recipes side dish recipe for Naga Style Chicken With Bamboo Shoot Recipe
reference_text:
**Naga Style Chicken With Bamboo Shoot Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
2 Tomatoes - crushed in pestle & mortar,Spring Onion (Bulb & Greens) - few,Salt - to taste,1 kg Chicken,2 inch Ginger - grated,5 cloves Garlic - crushed,7/8 Green Chillies - crushed,1/2 cup Bamboo shoots - sliced,1/2 cup Cabbage (Patta Gobi/ Muttaikose) - sliced

**Instructions:**
To prepare Naga Style Chicken With Bamboo Shoot Recipe, wash chicken pieces thoroughly and drain all the excess water.Heat a wok on medium flame, add the ginger, garlic, green chillies and saute for about 30 seconds.
To this add the cabbage, bamboo shoots and sprinkle some salt.
Cover and cook for about 2-3 minutes.
Once the cabbage has wilted down add the chicken and the tomatoes.Mix it well, sprinkle some water and cook covered, on a medium flame until chicken is tender and well cooked.
This will take about 20-25 minutes.
Garnish with spring onions and some bamboo shoot slices, optionally.Serve Naga Style Chicken With Bamboo Shoot Recipe with hot steamed rice and Hmarcha Rawt (chutney).
generated_text:
**Nagaland Style Chicken With Bamboo Shoot (Lushei) Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
2 teaspoons Red Chilli powder,1/4 cup Coriander Powder (Dhania),Salt - to taste,500 grams Boneless chicken breasts,Sunflower Oil - as required,5 cloves Garlic,250 grams Spring Onion Greens - chopped,300 gram Elephant yam,200 grams Bok choy - cut into half lengthwise & cleaned,10 Kokum (Malabar tamarind)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Nagaland Style Lushei Recipe , prep all your vegetables and keep it ready.In order of use; chop spring onions finely, peel and clean bamboo shoots by removing stem.
Wash them under running water thoroughly.Cut elephant yams in thick slices about ½ inch thickness.Heat oil in pressure cooker on medium heat; add garlic pods and saute until they turn golden brown.Add green chillies alongwith kokum pieces that have been soaked in hot water.Saute till soft.Then add sliced elephants yard which will take just one minute.Now add bok choy and cover cook for two whistles.After releasing natural pressure, open lid carefully.Open the cooker; remove excess moisture from leaves using kitchen towel.Pound together coriander seeds, red chili powders without adding any ghee or oil.Once done grind this mixture coarsely in mixer grinder.
Keep aside.Mix grounded spice mix with salted yogurt well.To pan, put boneless chicken breast pieces coated generously with ground paste, pour little more marinade over the chicken.Sprinkle some additional crushed black pepper if you want.Fry chicken pieces gently covered at low flame for couple minutes.Serve each piece separately garnished with fresh onion greens on top.Garnish cooked noodles/fried eggplant/banana flower buds/gourd etc., around.Nagaland's traditional meal also includes rice, steamed rice is optional but can be served when there are guests.The local cuisine has rich spices like Sichuan peppercorns called "Khasha" which adds special flavor while eating these dishes.If not available then substitute with regular whole peppercorn instead.Delicious Nagalnad style lusehi makes perfect Sunday lunch / dinner menu .

user_message: Generate a High Protein Vegetarian Coastal Karnataka lunch recipe for Muga Ambat Recipe
reference_text:
**Muga Ambat Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Fresh coconut - grated,1 cup Green Moong Sprouts,Water - as required,3 Dry Red Chillies,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,6 Dry Red Chillies,1 tablespoon Sunflower Oil,1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 Onion - thinly sliced,1 sprig Curry leaves,3 tablespoon Tamarind Water

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Muga Ambat recipe, Grind coconut, dried red chilies with little water into a paste.
Keep aside.Boil sprouted green gram in enough water with turmeric powder and little salt until it’s cooked.
The green gram should be tender but firm, do not make it mushy.Now add the ground coconut mix into the cooked green gram and continue to cook for about 15 minutes on medium flame.Add tamarind juice and continue to cook for another 8 minutes.In another pan heat oil.
First add onion slices and fry until golden brown.
Remove from flame keep aside.
Add mustard seeds and let it splutter.
Then add the dried red chilies, curry leaves, mix and turn off flame.Add the tempering to the curry.
Turn off the flame.
Serve Muga Ambat along with Whole Wheat Lachha Paratha and Bhindi Raita for a weekday lunch or dinner.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian snack recipe for Puffed Lotus Seed Pakoras Recipe - Makhana Pakora
reference_text:
**Puffed Lotus Seed Pakoras Recipe - Makhana Pakora**


**Ingredients:**
1 Onion - sliced,1 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - chopped,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1/2 cup Gram flour (besan),1 Green Chilli - chopped,1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 sprig Curry leaves - chopped,100 grams Phool Makhana (Lotus Seeds) - roasted in ghee

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Puffed Lotus Seed Pakoras Recipe, we will first make the pakora batter.In a mixing bowl, combine all the ingredients together -lotus seeds, besan flour,  onion, green chilli, cumin seeds, red chilli powder,  turmeric powder, salt, coriander leaves,curry leaves.
 Add water little at a time, to make a thick pakora batter.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Kerala Recipes lunch recipe for Chow Chow Thoran (Poriyal) Recipe
reference_text:
**Chow Chow Thoran (Poriyal) Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
2 Green Chillies,1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),2 sprig Curry leaves,Salt - to taste,Cumin seeds (Jeera),1/2 cup Fresh coconut - grated,3 Chayote

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Chow Chow Thoran Recipe, peel the chow chow and cut them into medium size dices.
Steam the vegetable with salt in the pressure cooker adding just about 2 tablespoons of water or a steamer until it is soft and cooked.In a blender, make a coarse mixture of the coconut, one sprig of curry leaves, green chillies and cumin seeds.Combine the coconut mixture into the steamed chow chow and keep aside.The final step is to make the seasoning.
Heat a small pan with a teaspoon of oil; add in the mustard seeds and allow it to crackle.Once it crackles, stir in the other sprig of curry leaves and the turmeric powder.Turn off the heat and pour this seasoning into the Chow Chow Thoran mixture.
Stir to come well and check the salt levels and adjust to suit your taste.You can serve Chow Chow Thoran along with Steamed Rice, Tomato Rasam, Mixed Vegetable Sambar and Elai Vadam for an everyday meal.

reference_text:
**Mushroom Curry Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 cup Tomato - puree,1 teaspoon Garam masala powder,1 Onion - cut into chunks,Salt - to taste,4 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,1 Green Bell Pepper (Capsicum) - cut into chunks,200 grams Button mushrooms - halved,1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,1 inch Ginger - finely chopped,Mint Leaves (Pudina) - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder

**Instructions:**
To begin making Mushroom Curry, first heat oil in a pan or wok and add onions, ginger, garlic and capsicum and saute until the onions soften.Next add the mushrooms and saute until mushrooms are cooked.
Once done add the tomato puree and the dry maslas like turmeric powder, red chilli powder, garam masala and salt.Bring the Mushroom curry to a brisk boil for two to three minutes.Stir in the chopped mint leaves and turn off the heat.
Check the salt and seasoning and adjust to suit your taste accordingly.
Transfer Mushroom Curry into a serving bowl.Serve Mushroom Curry for your lunch/ weeknight dinner or even at your parties with Pudina Tawa Paratha, Tadkewali Masoor Dal and Rose Greek Yogurt Dessert .
