user_message: Generate a High Protein Non Vegetarian North Indian Recipes appetizer recipe for Coriander Mint Chicken Tikka Recipe - Dhaniya Pudina Chicken Tikka Recipe
reference_text:
**Coriander Mint Chicken Tikka Recipe - Dhaniya Pudina Chicken Tikka Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
oil - as per use, 2 tsp lemon juice, 4 cloves garlic, 3 sprig mint, 2 green chillies, 5 sprig green coriander,250 grams chicken - chopped, 2 tbsp Hung curd, 1/2 tsp Ginger - grated, chaat masala powder - as per use, salt - to taste

**Instructions:**
To make Coriander Mint Chicken Tikka recipe, first clean and wash the chicken thoroughly.
Grind it small and make a hole with the help of fork.
Keep it separately.
To make the marination spices, add green coriander, mint, Hung curd, green chillies, ginger, garlic, salt and make a paste in a mixer grinder.
Add chicken to it and mix well.
Keep it in the fridge for 1 hour.
Now heat oil in a grill pan.
Add marinated chicken to it and mix well.
Cook well on all sides.
A light brown layer will start to form on the chicken and the hakiyan will also become soft.
Once cooked, drain in a bowl, add chaat masala, lemon juice and mix well.
Serve coriander mint chicken tikka recipe with yogurt mint chutney and vinegar onion for your house parties.
generated_text:

**Ingredients:**
1 tablespoon Ginger Garlic Paste,2 tablespoons Gram flour (besan),Sunflower Oil,Salt - to taste,4 Whole Black Peppercorns,Mint Leaves (Pudina) - small bunch,500 grams Boneless chicken breasts - cut into bite sized pieces,300 gram Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),Cumin powder (Jeera) - to sprinkle,250 grams Onion - chopped roughly,5 Cardamom Powder (Elaichi)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Dhania pudina chikkin tikka marination first clean wash and soak in water.
Drain all excess of water from it.Cut them straight across so they can cook evenly.Heat oil in heavy bottomed pan over medium heat; add ginger garlic paste alongwith cumin seeds and saute until fragrant.Add onions and fry till translucent.Now turn off flame transfer this mixture to mixer grinder jar and blend to make smooth paste.Grind cardamon pods as well now that its hot.Also grind mint leaves separately.Transfer back on heated pan and roast besan adding little salt at once.After about half minute you will notice aromas coming out.Switch off after 30 seconds.Mix curd & ground masala properly.To marinate place chicken inside bowl or plate.Mound other prepared mixtures onto each piece one by one.Let everything rest overnight before using.Serve chilled.Don't grill these tikkis since they might get burnt easily.You could also use charcoal if your gas is not working.Once ready serve warm with freshly sliced onions , corals and some cucumber slices too!

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Awadhi one pot dish recipe for Matar Ki Tehri Recipe - Fragrant rice cooked with spices and fresh peas
reference_text:
**Matar Ki Tehri Recipe - Fragrant rice cooked with spices and fresh peas**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Basmati rice,1 Dry Red Chilli,1/4 cup Tomatoes - sliced,1 cup Green peas (Matar),1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),Salt - to taste,4-5 Cloves (Laung),3 tablespoons Ghee,1/4 cup Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,1 tablespoon Whole Black Peppercorns,2 Black cardamom (Badi Elaichi),1/4 cup Onions - sliced,1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1/4 inch Ginger - finely grated

**Instructions:**
To begin making Matar Ki Tehri, wash and soak the rice in enough water for 1 hour before cooking.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Karnataka snack recipe for Yele Kadubu Recipe With Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney
reference_text:
**Yele Kadubu Recipe With Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney**


**Ingredients:**
Water - as required,1 tablespoon Tamarind Paste,Salt - to taste,3 tablespoons Ghee,1-1/2 cups Whole Wheat Flour,2 Red onion,5 tablespoons Jaggery - powdered,1/4 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),10 cloves Garlic,Sunflower Oil - as required,10 Dry Red Chillies - adjustable

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Yele Kadubu Recipe With Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney, we will firstly make the Yele Kadubu.Mix the wheat flour, salt, and ghee into a mixing bowl.
Mix everything and gradually add water to form a dough.
Once done, keep it aside for 10 minutes.
Take a lemon size dough and roll it like a phulka.
Fold it twice like a triangular shape and make triangles like this for the remaining dough too.Grease an idli steamer and put these Yele Kadubu into it.
Steam the Yele Kadubu for 8 to 10 minutes and they are ready to be served.The next step is to make the chutney for Yele Kadubu.Heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan.
Add onion, garlic, fenugreek seeds and dry red chillies and cook till the onions become soft and translucent.Switch off the heat and let the onion chutney mixture cool down a bit.
Add this mix into a mixie grinder and add jaggery, salt and tamarind and grind it to smooth paste.Do not add water to this Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney.
Once done, take the Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney out in a bowl and serve with the Yele kadubu.Serve Yele Kadubu  Recipe With Sweet And Spicy Onion Chutney on its own for breakfast or tea time snacks along with Filter Coffee and Masala Chai.

1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,4 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,Salt - to taste,8 Curry leaves - roughly torn into small pieces (optional),Sunflower Oil - as required,10 tablespoons Raw Peanuts powder (Moongphali) - roasted till crispy or raw peanuts powdered,Curry leaves - few sprigs,Water - as needed,5 Green Chillies - slit lengthwise,Mint Leaves (Pudina) - handful of fresh mint leaves tightly packed,Lemon juice - from half lemon,Peanuts (Raw) - some more peeled and sliced thin,Rock Salt - to taste,50 grams Tamarind Paste,300 grams Rice rava - hot cooked,Asafoetida (hing) - A pinch,Black Urad Dal (Split)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Kela Ka Nuosha Bhajiya, we will first make the tawa paratha dough.
In a mixing bowl add in all the wheat flour along with salt and water little at time adding it slowly using your fingers and knead until you get soft pliable yet firm dough.Add very less oil if you find that its too sticky else use just enough amount of ghee/oil while covering the container.Cover this mixture & allow them rest covered for about 15 minutes.Now divide equal portions out of each ball and roll one portion between your palm dusted with dry rice flour on rolling board.Later flatten these balls further by pressing down lightly using hand.Heat up a skillet over medium heat; smear a bit of oil/ghee evenly around.In turn place flattened dough’s onto heated pan.Flip after about ten seconds when they start puffing up.
After couple of flips flip again so cook both sides well.Take off once brown spots appear.When done keep aside.Similarly prepare remaining dosas.Remove the prepared dosa's from tawa and spread peanut paste across entire surface leaving tiny border.Once placed, sprinkle black pepper corns followed by curry leaf, salt, hing.Mix everything properly without disturbing.Know the next layer is going be filled with boiled kelas.Make sure that kels have cooled completely before filling the nuoshu.
Drizzle coconut oil over the top.Grease hands with butter/mustard oil/cold pressed mustard seed extract then stuff kelse inside nuoshus carefully such that no oozes out.Place stuffed nuushis back on preheated griddle.Cook nuushi from side turning occasionally like how roti/tortillas do.Serve warm immediately.Your delicious homemade Kala ka Nuosha Bhajji can also be served during evening snacks or even breakfast meal .Serve this mouth-watering Kela Ka Nuosha Bhaja alongside Panchmel dal , Phulkas and Steamed Rice for lunch or dinner.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian South Indian Recipes side dish recipe for Podi Potta Pachhai Kathirikai | Brinjal Stir Fry Recipe With Curry Powder
reference_text:
**Podi Potta Pachhai Kathirikai | Brinjal Stir Fry Recipe With Curry Powder**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Chana dal (Bengal Gram Dal),1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,Salt - to taste,1/2 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,15 Dry Red Chillies,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 cup Coriander (Dhania) Seeds,1/2 kg Small Green brinjal (green aubergine),1/2 cup White Urad Dal (Split),1 tablespoon Sunflower Oil,1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),Tamarind - a small ball,1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi)

**Instructions:**
To prepare Podi Potta Pachhai Kathirikai | Brinjal Stir Fry Recipe With Curry Powder, prepare curry powder first.
Meanwhile, soak the tamarind in 2 cups of water and set aside.Heat oil in a thick bottomed pan.
Add the mustard seeds.Add the remaining ingredients and stir fry on low, until the dals turn golden in colour.Allow to cool completely.Grind into a coarse powder in a mixer and you can store in an airtight jar.For the Podi Potta Pachhai Kathirikai | Brinjal Stir Fry Recipe With Curry Powder, Cut the brinjals lengthwise diagonally and then slice into bite sized pieces.Put the cut veggies in the tamarind water to avoid oxidization.Heat oil in a wide thick bottomed pan.
Add the mustard seeds and hing.Drain the tamarind water and add the sliced brinjals once the mustard seeds splutter.Mix in the turmeric powder, coriander powder and salt.Cover with a lid and cook on a low to medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes.Open cook for another 5 minutes once the veggies seem almost done.Add 2 tablespoons of the 'curry podi' prepared.Carefully toss the veggies such that the podi coats their surface evenly.Turn off the heat andServe Podi Potta Pachhai Kathirikai | Brinjal Stir Fry Recipe With Curry Powder along with phulka or steamed rice and sambar of your choice.

**Podi Potta Pachhai Kathirikai | Brinjal Stir Fry Recipe With Curry Powder**


**Ingredients:**
1 tablespoon Coriander (Dhania) Seeds,2 Green Chillies - slit lengthwise,Salt - to taste,4 Dry Red Chilli,5 cloves Garlic,500 grams Bhindi (Lady Finger/Okra),Sunflower Oil - as required,8 Shallots - chopped finely,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - few sprigs finely chopped,Tamarind Paste - lemon size ball,Asafoetida ( hing)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Podi Potta Pachhai Vengayam Varuval Recipe first dry roast coriander seeds in small pan until aromatic.
Take out from stove when it starts emitting aroma.Take them off heat.Add all these roasted spices into mixer-jar along with green chillis, garlic pods & grind them coarsely.Now add this powder on tawa or kadai over medium flame.Sprinkle some oil drop by drop so that no lumps form.Pour water little at time while stirring continuously till mixture thickens.If you find too watery then reduce the amount of water you can use.Next sprinkle salt according to your taste.Turn down flames now, cover lid slightly and cook further for about three minutes.Remove Kadhi after cooked.
Add tamarind paste, stir well.Allow to cool completely.Mix this podipodi masala together with cut brinjals / vengaya poosanikadali.Dry fry shallowly using little bit more than needed oil.
Sprinkle curry leaves, asafotida and mix everything properly.Serve hot.You may also store leftovers in refrigerator up-to one week.Garnish with fresh coriander leaves just before serving.Serve Podi potti Pooshakki Vengayam Variyal/Kathiri Kai Thoran/Sabzi along with Steamed Rice, Mixed Vegetable Sambar, Cabbage Thoran , Keerayi Puli Kuzhambu .

1/2 cup Mango (Raw) - diced,1/4 cup Green Chutney (Coriander & Mint),1/2 cup Pineapple - diced,1 cup Cucumber - diced,2 sprig Mint Leaves (Pudina) - roughly torn

reference_text:
**Lauki Badi Ki Sabzi (Recipe In Hindi)**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon garam masala powder, 1/2 teaspoon coriander powder, 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, 3 cloves garlic - grated, oil - 4 curry leaves, asafoetida - a pinch, 1/2 cup wood, 1 green chili - finely chopped , 1/2 teaspoon mustard, 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1/2 teaspoon cumin seeds, 1 inch ginger - grated,1/2 gourd - peel and chop

**Instructions:**
To make gourd elder greens, first roast the elder gourd in a frying pan.
Keep in mind that big burns do not happen.
After it is roasted, keep it to cool down.
Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds, mustard seeds and asafoetida.
After 20 seconds, add green chili and ginger kahasun.
After 1 minute add gourd and cook till it becomes soft.
After it gets soft add big roasted and mix it.
Now add red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder and salt.
After 10 seconds add milk to it and cook on low heat for 20 seconds.
Garnish with green coriander and serve hot.
Serve gourd elder vegetables with panchamel dal and tawa paratha for dinner.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian lunch recipe for Sweet Potato & Neem Leaves Vegetable Curry Recipe
reference_text:
**Sweet Potato & Neem Leaves Vegetable Curry Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 cup Neem leaves,1 Dry Red Chilli,Salt - to taste,4 Sweet Potatoes,1 tablespoon Roasted Peanuts (Moongphali) - powdered,1/3 teaspoon White Urad Dal (Split),1 teaspoon Chana dal (Bengal Gram Dal),1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),1/3 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1.5 tablespoon Sunflower Oil

**Instructions:**
To prepare Sweet Potato & Neem Leaves Vegetable Curry Recipe, clean the neem leaves and tear the stem out of it.Pressure cook the sweet potato for just 1 whistle or 5 minutes whichever is earlier, on full flame.
The pressure will take longer time if you put a lot of water.Take out, peel & chop them into cubes.Take a non-stick pan, add oil, hing, mustard, urad dhal, red chilli & bengal gram dal.
Fry them well.Add the neem leaves and let it fry for 3-4 minutes until they turn slightly crispy.Add the chopped sweet potato and salt.
Cook well until the sweet potato is fried well.Garnish with roasted peanut powder.Serve Sweet Potato & Neem Leaves Vegetable Curry Recipe with hot steaming rasam rice.

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Kandathalikai Mavu Palya Recipe by pressure cook tamarind water in one sauce pan till it becomes thin.
Keep aside.In another kadhai heat oil on medium flame add mustard seeds followed by dry red chillies.When they start spluttering pour garlic pods.Sauté them until golden brown.Now add pearl onion and saute well.Add curry leaves now after about two minutes later you can then add turmeric powder and mix everything properly.After few seconds, sprinkle some salt and let the mixture come together.At this stage when everything is mixed up nicely switch off the gas.Cool down completely and grind all of these along with fresh grated coconut using very little bit of warm or cold water.Transfer out into bowl.Mix both masala paste and cooked tamarind water in it.Make sure there should be no lumps.Once done add neem leaves into it and give stir so that the flavours get absorbed evenly.Finally garnish with coriander leaves and serve.Serve Kandathalikai Mavu palya hot with rice topped with ghee or just plain steamed rice .It makes excellent side dish too!

reference_text:
**Indian Style Kala Chana Salad Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
Salt - as required,1 Tomato - finely chopped,2 cups Kala Chana (Brown Chickpeas) - soaked overnight and boiled,1/2 teaspoon Black pepper powder,1 Onion - finely chopped,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped (adjustable),1 Lemon juice,1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves -

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Indian Style Black Chickpea Salad recipe, wash and soak the chana in water,and leave it overnight.
In a pressure cooker, add the chana and water and pressure cook for 6 whistles on high flame, reduce the flame and continue to cook for 15 more minutes.
Turn off the flame.

user_message: Generate a Diabetic Friendly North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Baingan Sarson Ka Saag Recipe
reference_text:
**Baingan Sarson Ka Saag Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 Onion - sliced,1 teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder),1 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,4 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),3 tablespoons Mustard oil - for cooking,200 grams Mustard greens,1 inch Ginger - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),300 grams Small Brinjal (Baingan / Eggplant) - quartered

**Instructions:**
To begin making Baingan Sarson Ka Saag Recipe first prep all the ingredients and keep aside.
Quarter the brinjal and keep them in salted water till you use them.
Keeping them in salted water prevents discolouration of the brinjal.Wash and chop the mustard greens ( sarson ka saag) roughly and keep them aside.
Next heat oil in a deep pan or wok and once the oil has reached a smoking point reduce the heat to medium.
Add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Then add onions, ginger and garlic and saute till the onions are cooked.Now add the drained and cut brinjal slices to the pan and saute.
Sprinkle some water, cover and cook the brinjal till its cooked through and is well roasted.This will take about 4 to 5 minutes.Once the brinjal is well roasted, softened and cooked through, add all the dry spices to the pan, chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder, amchur powder, salt and saute for a minute till all the brinjal is well coated with the masalas.
Finally add the chopped mustard leaves to the brinjals and cover and cook till the mustard greens are cooked through.
This will take about 3 to 4 minutes as well.Check the salt and seasonings of the Baingan Sarson Ka Saag and adjust according to taste.Once done, turn off the heat and transfer the Baingan Sarson Ka Saag to a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve Baingan Sarson Ka Saag with Phulka , Panchmel Dal and Lauki Raita for a lovely weekday lunch.

Serve Baigan masala sabzi with Dal Tadka Rice , Phulka and Papaya Raita for weekend meal.

user_message: Generate a North Indian Recipes recipe for Dahi Suran Ki Kadhi Recipe - Yam Cooked In Yogurt Curry
reference_text:
**Dahi Suran Ki Kadhi Recipe - Yam Cooked In Yogurt Curry**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,300 grams Elephant yam (Suran/Senai/Ratalu) - peeled and diced,1 tablespoon Gram flour (besan),Sunflower Oil - for cooking,1/2 teaspoon Whole Black Peppercorns - crushed,1/2 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),Salt - to taste,1/4 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),1/2 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder

**Instructions:**
We begin making the Dahi Suran Ki Kadhi Recipe (Yam Cooked In Yogurt Curry Recipe) by cooking the diced yam in the pressure cooker with 1/2 cup water and a little salt, pressure cook it for 4 whistles till it is soft and firm.
Let the pressure release naturally by itself.The next step is to make the Kadhi so for that add curd in a saucepan and then add 2 cups water and besan flour.
Keep mixing it on a low heat and bring it to a boil.
Add some salt, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander seeds, stir well and let it cook in low heat for few more minutes.
Later add in the boiled yam to the kadhi.
Once that is done, it is time to make the temper for the kadhi.
Heat a small shallow pan with oil and then add cumin seeds, cinnamon, bay leaf , fenugreek seeds and fresh ground peppercorns.Let the spices get roasted little and sprinkle the tempered spices on to the kadhi mixture.
Serve the Dahi Suran Ki Sabzi Recipe (Yam Cooked In Yogurt Curry Recipe) with Bhindi Masala, Jeera Rice and Phulka for an everyday meal.

user_message: Generate a South Indian Recipes recipe for Capsicum Masala Poriyal Recipe (South Indian Style Bell Pepper Stir Fry)
reference_text:
**Capsicum Masala Poriyal Recipe (South Indian Style Bell Pepper Stir Fry)**


**Ingredients:**
4 Dry Red Chillies,Salt - to taste,Curry leaves - Few,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),4 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - chopped,1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,3 Garlic - cloves,3 Red Yellow or Green Bell Pepper (Capsicum) - diced into cubes,1 tablespoon Fresh coconut,1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,1 Onion - diced into cubes,1/2 cup Roasted Peanuts (Moongphali)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Capsicum Masala Poriyal Recipe place a kadai on the heat.
Add some oil and warm it.Next, add in the mustard seeds and allow them to splutter.
Next, add the chopped onions and saute them until translucent.Next, toss in the capsicum, curry leaves, required salt and stir-fry for 5 minutes.In the mean time add all the ingredients under “For the Powder” in a blender and grind to a coarse powder.Add in the ground powder to the capsicum mixture and saute for 5 minutes until the capsicum is coated well with the spices.The capsicum pieces should have a slight crunch.
Do not overcook them.Turn off the heat and garnish with coriander.Serve hot with Egg Masoor Dal and Chapatis.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Maharashtrian Recipes indian breakfast recipe for Maharashtrian Gopalkala Recipe (Curd Poha With Fruits)
reference_text:
**Maharashtrian Gopalkala Recipe (Curd Poha With Fruits)**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Sugar,2 teaspoon Raw Peanuts (Moongphali),1/4 cup Cucumber - finely chopped,1/4 cup Anardana Powder (Pomegranate Seed Powder),5 Curry leaves - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 teaspoon Ghee,Salt - to taste,1/4 cup Fresh coconut - scraped,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1/2 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),1 cup Poha (Flattened rice)

**Instructions:**
To prepare Gopalkala Recipe (Curd Poha With Fruits), wash the Poha under running water and allow to drain in a colander.
(You can keep the colander on a fitting vessel.In a big mixing bowl, take this drained Poha and mix with chopped cucumber, grated Coconut, pomegranate seeds, curd, salt and sugar.Heat a tadka pan on medium heat and melt the ghee.Add cumin seeds, curry leaves, peanuts and chopped green chilies.Stir fry for a minute and pour this tempering over the Poha and mix well.Served Gopalkala Recipe for breakfast or as an afterschool snack for kids or for breakfast.

user_message: Generate a North Indian Recipes recipe for Phool Makhana Kheer Recipe - Sweetened With Jaggery
reference_text:
**Phool Makhana Kheer Recipe - Sweetened With Jaggery**


**Ingredients:**
2 tablespoons Pistachios - slivered,1/2 cup Jaggery - powdered,1-1/4 cups Lotus Seeds,3 tablespoons Badam (Almond) - slivered,1 teaspoon Cardamom Powder (Elaichi),1 tablespoon Ghee,2 teaspoons Dried rose petals - for garnish,1 liter Milk

**Instructions:**
To begin making Phool Makhana Kheer Recipe, we will firstly roast the makhanas.Heat ghee in a heavy bottomed pan, add the makhanas and slow roast it till it turns golden and crisp.
Keep it aside.Now, save a hand full of this roasted makhanas and pound the rest roughly using mortar and pestle.
Pour milk into a heavy bottomed pan, bring it to a boil on medium heat.
Boil the milk for 10 more minutes, till it becomes a little thicker.Now, add the pounded and whole roasted makhana's and mix well.
Simmer this mixture for 5 minutes, till it becomes creamy and soft.After 5 minutes, add in the powdered jaggery, cardamom powder, mix well and simmer till the jaggery is melted and well incorporated into the kheer.Switch off the heat, garnish with silvered nuts, dried rose petals and the Phool Makhana Kheer Recipe (Sweetened With Jaggery) is ready to serve.Serve the Phool Makhana Kheer Recipe as a part of the Festive Menu this Diwali and let your guests rejoice in the festive spirits.
You can also serve it as a dessert after your everyday meal of Paneer Butter Masala, Kachumber Salad and Tawa Paratha.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Himachal side dish recipe for Himachal Style Khatta Jimikand Recipe - Spicy Tangy Yam
reference_text:
**Himachal Style Khatta Jimikand Recipe - Spicy Tangy Yam**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Amchur (Dry Mango Powder),1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1/2 teaspoon Kala jeera,1 inch Ginger - chopped,1/2 teaspoon Kalonji (Onion Nigella Seeds),2 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,Mustard oil - for cooking,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 Onion - chopped,300 grams Elephant yam (Suran/Senai/Ratalu) - chopped small,1 sprig Curry leaves,1/2 teaspoon Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds),1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),4 cloves Garlic - chopped,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Himachal Style Khatta Jimikand Recipe, heat a pressure cooker with oil, add all the seeds that are mentioned above including cumin seeds, kalonji, methi seeds, kala jeera and mustard seeds.Allow it to crackle for few seconds.
Add in curry leaves and leave to splutter for few seconds.Add ginger and garlic and saute until they soften.
Add in onions and cook until they turn golden brown.Add chopped yam along with all the spice powders and salt to taste and give it a stir.Add about 1 cup of water and pressure cook the yam for atleast 3 whistle until it becomes soft.Once done switch off the heat and allow the pressure to release naturally and bring the cooker back on the heat.Open the lid and keep sauteing until the water has all evaporated and the subzi thickens.Serve the Himachal Style Khatta Jimikand Recipe along with Kadhi, Bhakri and Cauliflower & Cucumber Raita by the side for a delicious meal.

user_message: Generate a No Onion No Garlic (Sattvic) North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Singhara Pulao Recipe - Water Chestnut Mixed Rice
reference_text:
**Singhara Pulao Recipe - Water Chestnut Mixed Rice**


**Ingredients:**
Sunflower Oil,2 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - chopped,Salt - to taste,2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),2 teaspoon Black pepper powder,2 cups Cooked rice,300 grams Singhade (water chestnut) - pressure cooked and skin peeled,2 Cloves (Laung),1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 Star anise

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Singhara Pulao Recipe (Water Chestnut Rice Recipe), keep the cooked rice ready.Pressure cook the water chestnuts with water for at least 8 whistles and allow it to rest for few seconds until it releases pressure naturally.Once done, take the water chestnuts out and peel off the black skin and keep the see aside.
Chop them into half.In a kadai, heat some oil, add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cloves and star anise and allow it to sizzle.Add in cooked water chestnuts, salt to taste, turmeric powder and pepper powder.
Saute until they are well combined.
This will take about 2 minutes.After 2 minutes, add the cooked rice and toss it over gently until the rice is coated evenly and serve hot by sprinkle some chopped coriander leaves on top.Serve the Singhara Pulao Recipe along with a Palak Makhana Recipe and Makhana Moongphali Ki Kadhi to make it a complete meal.
generated_text:
**Singhara Seeds Rava Upma | Singhare Ke Daliya Ka Khichdi**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup singhare seeds,2 Green Chillies,Sunflower Oil,Ghee - as required,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - few sprigs finely chopped,4 cloves Cloves (Laung),Salt - to taste,10 Whole Black Peppercorns,Curry leaves - handful,Few Cashews - broken into halves,1/8 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Singhare Ki Dalicha or Singhura Vatana Khichdi first wash rice thoroughly.
Soak them separately in water for about half hour.Dry roast each of these over medium heat till they get fragrant on low flame.Once done, drain excess moisture from both using kitchen towel.In another pan add oil or ghee.Add cumin seeds and allow it splutter.Now break cashews, green chilies & whole black peppercorn between your palms.Cashews should not be completely crushed but just roughed up.Saute all this mixture along with curry leaves until you can smell aroma coming out.Lower the gas now, then add dry roasted rices slowly stirring continuously so that lumps don't form.Mix well.
Cook covered till everything is mixed properly.Add salt according to use.Serve hot with some coriander leaves sprinkled on top.
Serve Singhare ki dalicha / vatna khichi with Carrot Tomato Peanut Chutney or any other vegetable gravy like Rajasthani Laal Maas and Lauki Raita by side to enjoy Sunday brunch at home!

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Dhingri Matar Recipe (Mushrooms and Peas Spicy Curry)
reference_text:
**Dhingri Matar Recipe (Mushrooms and Peas Spicy Curry)**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),4 cloves Garlic,Sunflower Oil - for cooking,1 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,1 inch Ginger,1 Stalk Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,400 grams Button mushrooms,1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1/4 cup Green peas (Matar) - steamed,5 Cashew nuts,2 cups Homemade tomato puree,1 cup Soy Chunks (Nuggets),1 tablespoon Poppy seeds

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Dhingri Matar Recipe, we will first make a paste of the ginger and garlic, adding a dash of water if required.
Keep asideMake a paste of poppy seeds and cashew nuts, adding just enough water to make a paste.
Keep aside.Soak the soy chunks in warm water for about 15 minutes and keep aside.Heat the oil in a pan; add the ginger and garlic paste and saute until well absorbed into the oil and turned light golden brown.Add the tomato puree and turmeric powder and saute for a few minutes.
Stir in the peas, soy chunks, mushrooms and simmer until the mushrooms are tender.Add the garam masala, cashew nut-poppy paste, salt, chilli powder.
Simmer for about 5 minutes and bring the Dhingri Matar to a quick boil.
Check the salt and spice levels and adjust to suit your taste.
Turn off the heat and stir in the chopped coriander leaves.
Once done, transfer to a serving bowl and serve warm with your choice of naan

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian side dish recipe for Bhindi Raita Recipe (Okra Salad)
reference_text:
**Bhindi Raita Recipe (Okra Salad)**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 tablespoon Groundnut powder - roasted,1 teaspoon Sugar,1/2 teaspoon Mustard seeds,3/4 cup Hung Curd (Greek Yogurt) - whisked,Salt - to taste,1/2 teaspoon Chaat Masala Powder,1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),2 tablespoons Sunflower Oil,250 grams Bhindi (Lady Finger/Okra) - cleaned,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Bhindi Raita first, mix 1-1/2 tablespoon oil with the Bhindi and rub lightly to coat the slices.
Add some salt and half of the chilli powder.Microwave (in batches) for 5 to 6 minutes on high till crisp.
Stir at 1 minute interval so that they cook evenly.Alternatively you can pan fry of air fry the slices till crisp.
Cool it down.In a mixing bowl add the curd.
Add little salt, sugar, groundnut powder, chaat masala and mix.In a small wok/pan, heat oil.
Add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, red chilli powder and hing.Add the crisp okras in the curd and pour the tempering on the curd.
The raita is ready to be served.Serve Bhindi Raita with roti or Rice and Panchmel Dal for a light everyday meal.

user_message: Generate a Kerala Recipes recipe for Kerala Mutton Roast Recipe - Kerala Mutton Masala
reference_text:
**Kerala Mutton Roast Recipe - Kerala Mutton Masala**


**Ingredients:**
Salt - as per your taste,1/2 teaspoon Fennel seeds (Saunf),2 sprig Curry leaves,1 Tomato - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon Black pepper powder,3/4 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1/2 teaspoon Garam masala powder,1 Onion - finely chopped,6 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,2 inch Ginger - finely chopped,1/4 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),500 grams Mutton - with bone (adds flavour),1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),3 tablespoons Coconut Oil

**Instructions:**
To begin making Kerala Mutton Roast Recipe, wash and clean the mutton pieces well.
In a pressure cooker, combine the mutton pieces, pepper powder, salt, turmeric powder and mix well.Add 1 cup of water.
Close the pressure cooker and place on high flame.Once the first whistle blows, reduce the flame to low and then let it continue to be on low flame for another 30 minutes.
After 30 minutes, turn off the heat and allow the pressure to release naturally.
Transfer the mutton pieces in a bowl and keep the stock aside.
The stock will be utilized later.
In a deep pan or wok add coconut oil on medium heat.
Once the oil is hot, add sliced onion and saute for about 3-4 minutes until the onions become soft and translucent.Now add fennel seeds, ginger, garlic and curry leaves and let it cook for 2 minutes.
To the onion mix, add red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder salt and stir well.
Saute for a couple of minutes until the Kerala Mutton Roast masala comes together.Once the raw smell of the masala is gone, add the chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and mushy.
This will take about 2 to 3 minutes.At this stage add the mutton stock and the mutton pieces into this Mutton Roast masala.
Let this cook on medium to high flame until the Kerala Mutton Roast becomes semi dry.Once the Kerala Mutton Roast Recipe is done, turn off the heat, check the salt and spice levels and adjust to suit your taste.Serve Kerala Mutton Roast along with Kerala Style Pulissery Recipe (Mor Kuzhambu) and Steamed rice for a complete meal or can be eaten along with Rotis, Ghee Roast Dosa or Kerala Style Appams.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Maharashtrian Recipes snack recipe for Maharashtrian Style Namkeen Shankarpali (Savory Flaky Pastry Diamonds Recipe)
reference_text:
**Maharashtrian Style Namkeen Shankarpali (Savory Flaky Pastry Diamonds Recipe)**


**Ingredients:**
2 tablespoon Sooji (Semolina/ Rava),2 tablespoon Red Chilli powder,4 tablespoon Ghee,2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),2 cups Whole Wheat Flour,1 teaspoon Salt

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Namkeen Shankarpali, mix the flour, semolina, red chilli powder, salt, cumin seeds in a bowl.Heat the ghee till it is light hot and pour it into the flour mixture.
Carefully mix to form a crumbly texture.
Make sure you do not burn hand.Add little water and knead it into a soft dough, rest the dough covered in a warm place for at least 30 minutes.We will start rolling the dough on a flat surface, sprinkle little flour if you feel the dough is very sticky.
Roll it to about 2 centimeter thick.Using a knife cut the dough into small diamond shapes or any desired shape and arrange them on a tray to fry.Heat a kadai with oil to deep fry the Shankarpalis.
Once the oil is hot carefully drop them and fry till they become light brown.Using a skimmer take the shakarpalis out and place it on the tissue paper in order to drain out the oil.Serve the Namkeen Shankarpali along with hot Masala Chai or South Indian Filter Coffee to make your evening special.

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Shahi Tukda namakpare, first get all the ingredients ready.
I have used store bought frozen puffed pastry sheet which you can use too.In order to make it at home we will need to follow this easy recipe of homemade puff pastry.To prepare Puff Pastry MethodFirst prep up by taking out two cups flour in mixing bowl.Add salt, cold butter cubes and using your fingertips rub them together until mixture resembles coarse crumbs like bread crumb texture.Now add ice-cold water little bit at time adding while rubbing everything till dough forms into small pea size balls.Meanwhile take another large shallow baking dish filled with warm water.Heat oil on medium flame; once hot gently drop each ball slowly into boiling water if they rise immediately then its good otherwise let sit there for about ten minutes.Likewise keep doing till all is done.Take out the dough after 10 mins & pat lightly between clean muslin cloth.Dust floured surface generously and roll each portion evenly thin enough so that when folded into triangles comes flat.Make sure not to over work dough else lamination won't be possible.For cutting tips see video link below.Cut each triangle diagonally starting point where you started rolling.Following way cut rest also.Press press edges tightly and fold overlapping some more.Sprinkle crushed almonds over both sides of shankapuri before sealing well.Do same method for remaining portions.Grease a tray lined with parchment paper with oil/butter.Spread half the prepared pastries on the greased pan.Leave these pastries for sometime in fridge for atleast 30 min.After refrigeration remove trays carefully transfer onto oven preheated at 200 C.Once again grease parchment paper with ghee/melted butter.
Place second batch of shankapuris.Bake for twenty five minutes without covering.Remove trays occasionally to ensure even browning.Serve Shahi Tikdi style along with khus-khus ,Gulab jamun etc during tea break or evening snacks .If you want something different try serving Shahi tiki's with Masala Chaas made from ginger garlic green chillies yogurt and cumin instead of regular buttermilk.You could serve Shahi tikdes with Dhaniya pudina chutney or date-tamarind chutny recipe followed with masala chai for an afternoon party breakfast.
ref_name: Maharashtrian Style Namkeen Shankarpali (Savory Flaky Pastry Diamonds Recipe)
ref_instructions:
To begin making the Namkeen Shankarpali, mix the flour, semolina, red chilli powder, salt, cumin seeds in a bowl.Heat the ghee till it is light hot and pour it into the flour mixture.
Carefully mix to form a crumbly texture.
Make sure you do not burn hand.Add little water and knead it into a soft dough, rest the dough covered in a warm place for at least 30 minutes.We will start rolling the dough on a flat surface, sprinkle little flour if you feel the dough is very sticky.
Roll it to about 2 centimeter thick.Using a knife cut the dough into small diamond shapes or any desired shape and arrange them on a tray to fry.Heat a kadai with oil to deep fry the Shankarpalis.
Once the oil is hot carefully drop them and fry till they become light brown.Using a skimmer take the shakarpalis out and place it on the tissue paper in order to drain out the oil.Serve the Namkeen Shankarpali along with hot Masala Chai or South Indian Filter Coffee to make your evening special.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Indian lunch recipe for Crunchy Ginger Capsicum Rice Recipe
reference_text:
**Crunchy Ginger Capsicum Rice Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
Salt - to taste,2 teaspoon Butter,1 Red Bell pepper (Capsicum),2 cups Basmati rice - cooked,2 Green Chillies - finely chopped,1 Green Bell Pepper (Capsicum),2 inch Ginger - grated,2 teaspoon Sesame seeds (Til seeds) - mixed black and white,1 Yellow Bell Pepper (Capsicum)

**Instructions:**
To begin making Crunchy Ginger Capsicum Rice Recipe, let's get the basmati rice ready.
Wash the basmati rice in running tap water until clear and soak for 30 minutes.Now, take a big pan, fill it with 3.5 cups of water and bring it to a rolling boil.
Add the soaked and drained rice, salt to taste, close it with a lid and cook for 20 minutes on a low flame.
The rice should be cooked to al dente (firm to bite).
Switch off the flame, let it rest for 10 minutes.Fluff up the rice with a fork such it does not become lumpy after cooling and keep aside.Now heat a kadai with some butter and a teaspoon of oil.
Oil will avoid the butter getting burnt or turning brown.Add the finely chopped bell peppers and green chillies.
Sauté for 2 minutes.Add the grated ginger and give it a good stir.Now add 2 cups of cooked basmati rice, season with salt and gently toss it to bring everything together.Garnish with toasted black and white sesame seeds and switch off the flame.Serve the Crunchy Ginger Capsicum Rice for kids lunch box or with chicken/vegetable Manchurian to make an interesting weeknight dinner.

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Ragi Methi Muttai Poriyal Recipe first heat oil in a wok.
Once it is hot add mustard cumin seeds and let them splutter.Add curry leaf, hing powder and garlic pods and saute till they turn soft.Once done, now add green bell pepper pieces and stir fry until it becomes tender.Now add salt according to your requirement and mix well.After some time you will see that all water evaporated from capsicums.
Add ragi flour little by little while stirring continuously so there won't be any lumps.Gradually everything gets cooked once rice grains starts getting separated.Now add coriander leaves and give a gentle toss.When you feel that texture of poriyal has reached desired consistency then switch off flame.Transfer into bowl.Serve this healthy ragi methi muttai pachadi along with steamed rice topped up with ghee during winters.Serve Crunchy Ragi Methi Muttaikose Pachdi on its own for breakfast too if you have no other plans about what can eat for dinner.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Gujarati Recipes snack recipe for Vatana Bateta Na Rotla Recipe - Gujarati Peas and Potato Roti
reference_text:
**Vatana Bateta Na Rotla Recipe - Gujarati Peas and Potato Roti**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Sugar,2 teaspoon Lemon juice,3/4 cup Rice flour,1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),Salt - to taste,1/4 cup Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,1 cup Green peas (Matar) - coarsely ground,2 inch Ginger - grated,1 tablespoon Sesame seeds (Til seeds),4 Potatoes (Aloo) - boiled and grated,1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),Sunflower Oil - as needed,1/2 cup Fresh coconut - finely grated,3 Green Chillies - finely chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Vatana Bateta Na Rotla Recipe, in a mixing bowl, add the rice flour and add 1 tablespoon oil to it.
Using your hand rub the oil to the flour to make a coarse crumb like mixture.Add the grated potatoes, grated coconut, green chili, ginger, coriander leaves, lemon juice, sesame seeds, sugar, salt, turmeric powder and asafetida.
Combine all the ingredients, check the salt and adjust to suit your taste.
Now add the coarsely ground peas to the mixture and give it a gentle mix.Place a flat non stick skillet pan on medium heat and add 1 or 2 tablespoons oil.
Once the oil heats up, put the Vatana Bateta No Rotla mixture in the pan and flatten it down so that the mixture fills up the pan, touches the sides of the pan.Allow it to cook so that it forms a golden crust.
once you notice the rotla is getting set and is getting baked in the pan, using a flat spatula and flip to cook on the other side.
(It's tricky but you should be able to get it right with practice).Let the crust form on the other side as well.
If necessary add few drops of oil on the sides of the Rotla.We are looking for even golden crust on all sides, once that is done allow the Rotla to rest for a few minutes in the pan.Transfer the Vatana Bateta Na Rotla to a plate and serve warm by cutting into squares or wedges.Serve the Vatana Bateta Na Rotla Recipe qualifies to be an absolutely guilt free tea time snack along with Green Chutney and Date Tamarind Chutney  and a cup of Masala Chai.
generated_text:
**Vatana Bateta Na Rotla Recipe - Gujarati Peas and Potato Roti **


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Kabuli Channa (White Chickpea Garbanzo Beans) - boiled,2 tablespoons Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,Salt - to taste,Ghee - or oil as required,Khus Khas Masala Powder - a pinch (optional),Water - to knead the dough,4 cloves Garlic - grated,Asafoetida (hing) - A small pinch of hing powder,Nutralite Classic Spread - spread on toast,5 Potatoes (Aloo)

**Instructions:**

To begin making Vatan na batata rotle, first prep all the ingredients.
Boil water in a saucepan; add potatoes into it and cook till they become soft.Once done drain out excess water from them and mash well using your hands.Heat ghee/oil in pan, then saute garlic until lightly browned.Add mashed potato mixture alongwith kikki beans & seasonings like salt, coriender leaves & khus khas masala powder if you want.Pour little water so that there should be no dry spots left.Cover lid for few minutes.After some time when everything is combined properly turn off flame.
Spread this stuffing over one side of each chapathi dough roll and close the sides completely.Now heat tawa; smear Nutralite classic spread evenly on both roasting pans.Flip after couple of seconds and roast the rolled dosa's in medium high flames until golden brown.Take care not to burn the bottom while flipping the rolled parathas.Serve hot.
Serve these delicious healthy Vatan na batate rotlas during tea-time break.

user_message: Generate an Uttarakhand-North Kumaon recipe for Kumaoni Style Palak & Methi Ka Kapa Recipe
reference_text:
**Kumaoni Style Palak & Methi Ka Kapa Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Sugar,4 Dry Red Chillies,1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 teaspoon Mustard oil,1 cup Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves),1/4 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),1 tablespoon Rice flour,2 cups Spinach

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Kumaoni Style Palak & Methi Ka Kapa Recipe, wash the greens in water, drain all the water and chop them finely.Heat a sauce pan with mustard oil over medium heat; add cumin seeds and dry red chillies and allow them to splutter for few seconds.
Add the asafoetida and saute for a few seconds.
Add the chopped Spinach and Methi leaves into the pan and stir fry until it softens.Once the Palak & Methi becomes soft, sprinkle rice flour, salt, turmeric powder, red chilli powder and sugar and saute the Palak & Methi Ka Kapa on high heat.The rice flour will absorb the excess water and help thicken the Palak & Methi Ka Kapa.
Once the Palak & Methi Ka Kapa thickens, turn off the heat.Check the salt and seasonings and adjust to suit your taste.
Transfer the Palak & Methi Ka Kapa to a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve the Kumaoni Style Palak & Methi Ka Kapa Recipe along with Steamed rice, Punjabi Rajma Masala Recipe and papad by the side.

**Kumaoni Style Palak & Methi Ka Kapa Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1/4 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),2 Green Chillies,Water - as required,Salt - to taste,15 grams Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,Coriander (Dhania) Seeds,500 gram Spinach Leaves (Palak)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Kamauni style palak aur methi ka kappo recipe by cleaning spinach leaves in water.
Wash them thoroughly under running tap water.
Drain excess water from it.Now chop roughly and keep aside.Heat a pan on medium flame; add oil into it.Add coriander seeds and green chillis and roast until they turn brown.Once done switch off heat and allow this mixture cool down.To grind paste we will need fresh ground coriander-seed mix along with some salt and yogurt.Grind coarsely using little water adding very less at time so that you don’t make too watery gravy.Add freshly washed spinach to grinder jar and pulse again till smooth consistency is achieved.Strain the puree through cheesecloth or muslin cloth to remove out any remaining chunky bits.Stir well once more if needed and adjust seasoning accordingly.Serve hot with phulka and masala tea for your evening snack or breakfast meal.

user_message: Generate an Indian recipe for Spicy Rice Stuffed Paratha Recipe
reference_text:
**Spicy Rice Stuffed Paratha Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Cumin powder (Jeera),2 Green Chillies,Sunflower Oil - or ghee for cooking paratha,1/2 cup Cooked rice - (leftover),Water - as required,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 Onion - finely chopped,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - small bunch,1 cup Whole Wheat Flour,2 teaspoons Lemon juice,1 teaspoon Chaat Masala Powder

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Spicy Rice Stuffed Paratha, we will first make the dough.
Add in the flour and a pinch of salt into a large bowl.
Add a little water at a time and knead to make a firm yet smooth dough.Knead well for about 3 to 4 minutes, this will make the parathas soft.Drizzle a teaspoon of oil over the dough and knead for another couple of minutes.
Cover and keep aside till we make the rice filling for the paratha.Next, we will combine the leftover rice, chopped onions, green chillies, coriander leaves, red chilli powder, chaat masala, cumin powder, lemon juice and salt.
Stir to combine the ingredients well.Now to begin making the Spicy Rice Stuffed paratha, keep some flour aside for dusting.
Divide the dough into large lemon side balls.Dust the dough portions in flour, flatten it and roll it into a 3 inch diameter circle.
Place the rice filling on half side of the circle and close the paratha by folding it with the other half.
Yes its as simple as that.
We will have a semi circle oval shape.Press the sides of the paratha down to seal the edges and gently using the rolling pin to flatten it.
Take care, the filling can ooze out if the dough is rolled out too thin.Now to cook the Spicy Rice Stuffed Paratha, first preheat the skillet on medium heat.
Place the rolled paratha on top and allow it to cook on one side.
After about 10 to 15 seconds, flip the paratha.At this stage, smear some oil or ghee to cook the paratha.
Cook the paratha by flipping over a couple of times until you see golden brown spots on both sides.Proceed the same way with the remaining dough portions and it is ready to be served.
Serve Spicy Rice Stuffed Paratha with Dhaniya Pudina Chutney and Burani Raita for a healthy breakfast.

user_message: Generate a Chettinad recipe for Chettinad Chicken Curry Recipe - Chettinad Chicken Surri
reference_text:
**Chettinad Chicken Curry Recipe - Chettinad Chicken Surri**


**Ingredients:**
3 Green Chillies,4 Dry Red Chillies,1 tablespoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),2 tablespoon Red Chilli powder,1/2 teaspoon Fennel seeds (Saunf),2 sprig Curry leaves,1 Tomatoes - chopped,1 teaspoon Black pepper powder,Salt - to taste,3 tablespoon Sunflower Oil,Water - as required,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - chopped for garnish,500 grams Chicken,1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi) - + 1/2 teaspoon extra,1 teaspoon Fennel Powder - roasted,1 tablespoon Ginger Garlic Paste,1/2 cup Tamarind Water,1-1/2 cup Shallots - chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Chettinadu Kuzhambu recipe, clean the chicken thoroughly.
Then add about 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder and water until chicken in fully immersed and let it sit for 10 minutes.
Then drain the turmeric water and marinate with little salt.
This step of washing chicken in turmeric water will reduce the smell in the chicken.In a wok/kadai, add oil on medium flame.
First add finely chopped shallots and sauté till onions turn soft.

user_message: Generate a Gujarati Recipes recipe for Pudina Sooji & Oats Dhokla Recipe - Steamed Semolina Cake With Oats & Mint Leaves
reference_text:
**Pudina Sooji & Oats Dhokla Recipe - Steamed Semolina Cake With Oats & Mint Leaves**


**Ingredients:**
1 Ginger - grated,1 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),1/4 cup Mint Leaves (Pudina) - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Sesame seeds (Til seeds),Red Chilli powder - for garnish,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Mustard seeds,1 teaspoon Sunflower Oil,1/2 teaspoon Asafoetida (hing),2 cups Sooji (Semolina/ Rava),40 grams Rolled Oats Or Instant Oats,2 teaspoons Enos fruit salt,1 sprig Curry leaves

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Pudina Sooji & Oats Dhokla Recipe, we will first roast the semolina (sooji).In a wok, dry roast the semolina until you get a slightly roasted flavor.
Roast on medium heat in a kadai for about 3 to 4 minutes taking care not to brown the semolina (sooji).
Once roasted, transfer to a large mixing bowl and keep aside.In a large mixing bowl where you have kept the roasted semolina, add the oats, the asafoetida, the mint leaves, the ginger, the salt, the teaspoon of oil and yogurt.
Add water little at a time and stir to make a thick batter (about 2-1/2 cups).
Check the salt and adjust to suit your tastePudina Sooji Oats Dhokla batter to rest for about 10 minutes.Once the batter is well rested, add the eno fruit salt and mix vigorously.
The next step is to steam the Pudina Sooji & Oats Dhokla.
Preheat a steamer with water on medium-high heat.
Grease the Dhokla Plates with oil and pour the Pudina Sooji & Oats Dhokla batter evenly into the plates.
Fill it only up to 3/4th level of the plates.
Sprinkle just a little red chilli powder over the dhokla batter to add to the taste of the dhoklaPlace the filled dhokla plates in the steamer and steamer on high for 10 minutes.
After 10 minutes turn off the heat and remove the dhokla from the steamer and set aside for 5 minutes before you can cut them into shapes.The final step is to season the Pudina Sooji & Oats Dhokla.
Heat a teaspoon of oil in a tadka pan; add the mustard and cumin seeds and allow it to crackle.
Add the curry leaves and give it a stir.
Turn off the heat.Drizzle the seasoning over the Pudina Sooji & Oats Dhokla and serve it along with Green Chutney for breakfast or dinner

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Awadhi lunch recipe for Subz Noor E Chashm Recipe (Nawabi Curry Recipe )
reference_text:
**Subz Noor E Chashm Recipe (Nawabi Curry Recipe )**


**Ingredients:**
1 Cauliflower (gobi) - cut into small florets and blanched,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder -,3 Cloves (Laung) - 3 cloves,1 inch Ginger - chopped,Sunflower Oil - for cooking,2 teaspoons Garam masala powder,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 Carrot (Gajjar) - cubed and blanched,1 Bay leaves (tej patta),Salt - taste,2 Onions - chopped,1 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - for garnish,3 cloves Garlic - chopped,2 Tomatoes - chopped,2 tablespoons Badam (Almond) - blanched,1/2 cup Green peas (Matar),2 tablespoons Cashew nuts,1/2 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera)

**Instructions:**
We begin making the Subz Noor E Chashm Recipe (Nawabi Curry Recipe ) by making the paste for the curry, heat a non stick pan and crackle the cumin seeds.
Add onions and saute till it is translucent and golden brown.Later on add the chopped tomatoes, cook till it becomes mushy and soft.
Add in the nuts and saute for 2 more minutes.
Remove it from the flame and let it cool so you can blend it into a smooth paste using a blender.Once it is done mix it with curd and combine it evenly.Next is to make the curry, heat a flat bottomed pan and pour some oil, add in the cumin seeds, ginger and garlic.
Saute till it is brown.
Add all the vegetables and saute for about 10 minutes.Once the vegetables are cooked little,add the ground paste and coat it evenly.
Add the spice powders that are listed and cook again for 4 more minutes.You can adjust the consistency by adding some water and bring it to a boil.
Check for seasoning, according to your taste.Serve the Subz Noor E Chashm Recipe (Nawabi Curry Recipe ) along with whole wheat Lachha Paratha to enjoy the Awadhi Meal.
generated_text:
**Subz Noor e Chasam | Nawabpur Style Vegetable Korma Recipe **


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),2 tablespoons Raisins,8 Cashew nuts - halved,Salt - to taste,250 grams Green beans (French Beans) - chopped finely,4 cloves Garlic - crushed between palms of hands or grated using a grater,5 Whole cashews,500 ml Coconut milk,Ghee - as required,15 Badam (Almond)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Subzi-Noor-e-Chasem (Nawabsi Sabut Moong Dal Matar Ki Kurma/Recipe first make paste out of almonds by soaking them in hot water.
Drain excess water from soaked almond.Similarly prepare coconut milk without adding any salt.Now heat ghee in a heavy bottomed pan over medium flame.Add whole spices like cumin seeds, cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, bay leaves and let it splutter.Once they have done add onions and saute till golden brown.Next add garlic, green chillies, turmeric powder and mix well.Let this cook until onion turns light pinkish red color.After that add cubed baby potatoes along with some extra water, cover lid and cook on low flame for about ten minutes.When all the vegetables becomes tender enough drain off most of the water if there is too much liquid left then remove cooked veggies.Adjust seasoning according to your preference by sprinkling little bit more sugar, salt and lemon juice into potato.Heat up remaining ghee in another kadai/wok and shallow fry sliced almonds and raisans separately.Cool down slightly and grind together coarsely.Pour thick puree made from ground masala mixture directly onto subji.Mix gently so no lumps occur.Serve delicious Nawa bsi style vegetable korma with phulka and jeera rice or tandoori rotis and boondi raita for weekend meal.

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian Goan Recipes lunch recipe for Goan Style Brinjal Bharta Recipe
reference_text:
**Goan Style Brinjal Bharta Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 tablespoon Tamarind Paste,1 Brinjal (Baingan / Eggplant),Salt - to taste,1 Tamarind - marble sized ball,2 Onions - finely chopped,1/2 cup Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - finely chopped,1 tablespoon Sunflower Oil,2 Green Chillies - chopped,2 tablespoon Fresh coconut - grated

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Goan Style Brinjal Bharta Recipe, clean the brinjal and make 4 to 5 slits keeping the brinjal intact.Heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan.
Switch off the pan and apply this oil to the brinjal.
Once done, put the brinjal in the same pan and cover it with a lid.After 2 minutes, open the lid and turn the brinjal carefully to the other side.
Once it is slightly cooked from the other side, carefully insert the marble sized tamarind ball through one slit and again cover the lid.Keep on turning the brinjal till it gets cooked on all sides.
This might take 25 to 35 minutes.Once cooked, it will turn brown and will release water.
Let the brinjal remain covered until it cools down.After cooling, peel the brinjal and discard the peel.
Mash the pulp and mix it with finely chopped onions and green chillies in a bowl.Add grated coconut, tamarind paste, salt to taste and mix everything well.
Once done, add finely chopped coriander leaves, mix well and it is ready to be served.Serve Goan Brinjal Bharta Recipe along with Steamed Rice, Goan Moolyacho Ross and Amlechi Uddamethi Recipe for a comforting and delicious lunch.

user_message: Generate an Indian recipe for Methi Sprouts Salad Recipe - Healthy & Tasty Diabetic Snack
reference_text:
**Methi Sprouts Salad Recipe - Healthy & Tasty Diabetic Snack**


**Ingredients:**
1 tablespoon Lemon juice,Salt - to taste,1 Tomato - finely chopped,1/2 teaspoon Black pepper powder,1 cup Methi Seeds (Fenugreek Seeds) - sprouts,1/2 teaspoon Chaat Masala Powder,1 Onion - finely chopped,1/3 cup Peanuts,2 sprig Coriander (Dhania) Leaves

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Methi Sprouts Salad Recipe, we need to have the methi sprouts ready.
You can either use store bought methi sprouts or learn How to make Sprouts at Home.
You could also soak methi seeds in water overnight.
Next day, drain off the water and tie them in a muslin cloth and keep in a warm place for 1 day.After 24 hours, open the cloth and you will see the sprouts.
In a mixing bowl, combine the methi sprouts along with the peanuts, chopped onions and tomatoes, coriander leaves and mix well.
Then Sprinkle some salt, pepper and chaat masala and a generous squeeze of lemon and stir to combine to make the Methi Sprouts Salad.Transfer the Methi Sprouts Salad to a platter and serve as a diabetic snack or even as a side dish for lunch.Serve Methi Sprouts Salad along with Ragi Tawa Paratha, Methi Moong Dal Recipe, Masala Karela Recipe and Mooli Raita Recipe for a complete Diabetic Snack, lunch or dinner.

user_message: Generate a High Protein Vegetarian Karnataka side dish recipe for Karnataka Style Menthya Soppu Hesaru Kaalu Palya Recipe
reference_text:
**Karnataka Style Menthya Soppu Hesaru Kaalu Palya Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 Dry Red Chilli,2 teaspoons Sunflower Oil,Salt - to taste,1 Tomato - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 Onion - finely chopped,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - a few sprigs,2 cups Green Moong Sprouts - steamed,3 cloves Garlic,1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 cup Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves) - roughly chopped,1 teaspoon Jaggery - powdered,4 Shallots - chopped

**Instructions:**
To begin making Menthya Soppu Hesaru Kaalu Palya Recipe, pressure cook the sprouted green gram for one whistle adding a cup of water using a pressure cooker and keep aside.Make a course mix of the shallots, garlic cloves, cumin seeds and dry red chilli using a mortar and pestle and keep aside.Heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan, once the cooking oil is hot, add the onions over medium heat and saute until they turn translucent.Add in the pounded masala mix and saute until the raw smell of the garlic and shallots disappears.Once the mixture is smelling cooked, add the chopped tomatoes and cook until it turns mushy.After the tomatoes are a bit mushy, add the fenugreek leaves, turmeric powder, season with required salt to taste and mix well by sauteing.Let the fenugreek leaves cook to 3/4th, and occasionally keep stirring the mix.
You can ad a bit of water if you need, at this stage.Occasionally keep stirring on low medium heat, until the methi/fenugreek leaves are mostly done.
Add in the cooked sprouted green gram, jaggery powder and mix well.Let the mix simmer for about 5 minutes or till done and let the water evaporate if any.Garnish with coriander leaves, mix well and switch off the heat.Serve Menthya Soppu Hesaru Kaalu Palya Recipe (Karnataka Style Fenugreek Leaves and Green Gram Stir Fry) along with Akki Rotti Recipe (Spicy Gluten Free Rice Flour Flat Breads), Karnataka Style Badanekai Yennegai Gojju Recipe and Indian Vegetable Salad Recipe.

**Karnataka Style Menthya Soppu Hesaru Kaali Palya Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1/4 cup Arhar dal (Split Toor Dal),2 cloves Garlic,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania) - to sprinkle,Salt - as per taste,300 grams Spinach Leaves (Palak) - chopped,Turmeric powder (Haldi) - A pinch,15 Dry Red Chillies,Water - Salt,10 Shallots - finely sliced,Asafoetida (hing) - A pinch,Curry leaves - few sprigs,Few cashews - roasted,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - Handful,Coconut Oil - As required,5 tablespoons Fresh coconut - grated,1 tablespoon Jaggery,12 Small Brinjal (Baingan / Eggplant)

**Instructions:**

To begin making the Karnataka Style Menthya Soppu Hesarekai Palya Recipe we will first cook brinjals in pressure cooker.
Add all the vegetables along with salt, water into the pressure cooker.Add half of the turmeric power, jaggery and close it with lid.Pressure cook them till you hear two whistles.Turn off the heat after one whistle, let the steam release naturally from the pan.Open the lid carefully, add some more oil if needed.The next is to make masala paste.Heat a small skillet on low flame; roast garlic until they turn brownish golden.Then grind together shallot, dry red chillies, coriander seeds using little bit of hot water adding very less amount to avoid over flow.Transfer this mixture back to saucepan which has boiled brinjals.Sprinkle hing, remaining spices including curry leaf, salt.Mix everything well and simmer for about five minutes.Now mix fresh grated coconut to above mixture.
Gently fold in spinach without mashing.Give your final touch by sprinkling crushed nuts at last.Serve Hot or cold as desired.Karnataka Style Menthya soppu hesarikayi palya makes great accompaniment when served with steamed rice topped up with ghee.
ref_name: Karnataka Style Menthya Soppu Hesaru Kaalu Palya Recipe
ref_ingredients: 1 cup Methi Leaves (Fenugreek Leaves) - roughly chopped
ref_instructions:
To begin making Menthya Soppu Hesaru Kaalu Palya Recipe, pressure cook the sprouted green gram for one whistle adding a cup of water using a pressure cooker and keep aside.Make a course mix of the shallots, garlic cloves, cumin seeds and dry red chilli using a mortar and pestle and keep aside.Heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan, once the cooking oil is hot, add the onions over medium heat and saute until they turn translucent.Add in the pounded masala mix and saute until the raw smell of the garlic and shallots disappears.Once the mixture is smelling cooked, add the chopped tomatoes and cook until it turns mushy.After the tomatoes are a bit mushy, add the fenugreek leaves, turmeric powder, season with required salt to taste and mix well by sauteing.Let the fenugreek leaves cook to 3/4th, and occasionally keep stirring the mix.
You can ad a bit of water if you need, at this stage.Occasionally keep stirring on low medium heat, until the methi/fenugreek leaves are mostly done.
Add in the cooked sprouted green gram, jaggery powder and mix well.Let the mix simmer for about 5 minutes or till done and let the water evaporate if any.Garnish with coriander leaves, mix well and switch off the heat.Serve Menthya Soppu Hesaru Kaalu Palya Recipe (Karnataka Style Fenugreek Leaves and Green Gram Stir Fry) along with Akki Rotti Recipe (Spicy Gluten Free Rice Flour Flat Breads), Karnataka Style Badanekai Yennegai Gojju Recipe and Indian Vegetable Salad Recipe.

user_message: Generate a North Indian Recipes recipe for Arab Dahi Wali Sabz rode
reference_text:
**Arab Dahi Wali Sabz rode**


**Ingredients:**
1 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),1 1/2 teaspoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder,1 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,250 grams Colocasia root (Arbi),1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1 cup Water - adjustable,Sunflower Oil - to cook,1/2 teaspoon Ajwain (Carom seeds),1/4 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),2 tablespoon Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - chopped

**Instructions:**
To start preparing Arbi Dahi Wali Sabzi, scrub arbi to get rid of mud and dirt.
Wash it twice at least.
Pressure cook arbi with little water and a pinch of salt for 1 whistle (do not overcook).
Arbi has to be cooked, but firm.Peel the skin off and cut them into half, lengthwise.Whisk yogurt or dahi well, keep aside.Heat oil in a pan.
Add ajwain and sauté for 10 seconds.Add red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder and give it a mix.Add arbi and cook in oil and spices for about 10 to 12 minutes on medium flame with constant stirring.Add 1/2 cup water and bring it to boil.
Let it boil for 5 to 7 minutes.Add the whisked curd and with constant stirring, cook until the gravy just begins to come to a boil.Add garam masala powder, chopped coriander leaves, stir well and turn off flame.Serve Arbi Dahi Wali Sabzi along with Pudina Tawa Paratha, Mixed Sprouts Sabzi Recipe and Kachumber Salad for a perfect meal.

**Arab Dahi Wali Sabz Rode**


**Ingredients:**
1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),4 cloves Garlic - finely chopped,500 grams Spinach Leaves (Palak) - washed & roughly torn,Salt - to taste,Water - as required,Ghee - or oil for tempering,250 ml Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - few sprigs finely chopped,Red Chilli flakes - according to your spice preference,Mint leaves (Pudina) - small bunch ,Asafoetida (hing) - A pinch,Lemon juice - from one lemon,1 inch Ginger - grated,300 grams Green peas (Matar)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Palak Ki Kadhi Masala Recipe first get all the prep work done.
Wash spinach well in water.Pressure cook green chillies, ginger garlic together along with little salt till it becomes soft.Once cool peel off skin of boiled green chilies by peeling them between thumb and forefinger.In a pressure cooker add soaked moong dal, turmeric powder, salt and enough water just above half way.Cook until you hear two whistles on high heat.Switch off flame once its cooked through.Allow pressure release naturally.After releasing natural steam open lid, mash using masher into coarse mixture.Mix this masalai into yogurt thoroughly.Add freshly squeezed lime juice and mix properly.Heat ghee/oil in tadka pan over medium heat; add hing and let it splutter for about 10 seconds.Now pour hot tempered mustard seeds over kadhai wale palak ki kaddu and give stir gently.Finally sprinkle coriander leaves and mint and serve.Serve immediately without garnishing but if wish then some fresh pudina can be sprinkled on top Serve chilled or at room temperature.Taste wise I found that adding less chili flakes makes it more delicious while still spicy.If you prefer spicier version increase the quantity of red chilly flakes accordingly.Serve Arbi Ka Kadshe Puddine with Bhindi Tamatar Ki Sabzi, Phulka's and Steamed Rice for weekday lunch meal.

user_message: Generate a Tamil Nadu recipe for Kothu Kari Kuzhambu Recipe - Mutton Keema Curry
reference_text:
**Kothu Kari Kuzhambu Recipe - Mutton Keema Curry**


**Ingredients:**
1 Green Chilli - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,2 Onions - finely chopped,2 Tomatoes - finely chopped,2 Dagad phool (/pathar phool/black stone flower),1 teaspoon Garam masala powder,Salt - to taste,2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds,1/4 Mint Leaves (Pudina),300 grams Mutton Kheema,1 tablespoon Sunflower Oil,2 Cloves (Laung),2 teaspoons Coriander Powder (Dhania),1/4 cup Coriander (Dhania) Leaves,1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),1 tablespoon Ginger - finely chopped,1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1 Star anise,1 tablespoon Garlic - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Black pepper powder

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Kothu Kari Kuzhambu Recipe/ Mutton Keema Curry,  in a pressure cooker, heat oil on medium flame.
Once the oil is hot, add in all the whole spices -  bay leaf, cloves, cinnamon, cardamoms, star anise, stone flower petals and allow it to sizzle.
To this add the ginger, garlic and green chilli.
Next add in the onions and cook until transparent.
Once the onions are well cooked, add in the tomatoes and cook until mushy.
Once the onion-tomato mix has come together, add in the spices - red chilli powder, garam masala powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder,  pepper powder, coriander leaves, mint leaves, mutton keema and salt to taste.
Mix well and close the pressure cooker.
 Pressure cook the Kothu Kari Kuzhambu for 2 whistles and turn off the flame.
Allow the pressure to release naturally, then open the cooker.
Check the salt and seasonings and adjust according to taste.
Transfer the Kothu Kari Kuzhambu to a serving bowl and serve hot.Serve Kothu Kari Kuzhambu along with Steamed Rice or eat it along with Ghee Roast Dosa Recipe - Crispy Paper Dosa Recipe for a simple yet delicious meal.

**Ingredients:**
1 Onion - sliced,4 Dry Red Chillies,Salt - to taste,2 tablespoons Coriander Powder (Dhania),Sunflower Oil - as required,Mint Leaves (Pudina) - small bunch,Coriander (Dhania) Leaves - chopped,Curry leaves - few sprig,Tamarind Water - from one gooseberry sized lemon size ball of tamarind,250 grams Onions - finely chopped ,5 cloves Garlic - grated,500 gram Boneless mutton - minced,Water - or coconut milk for thickening the curry if needed,50 grams Tamarind Paste,Few Green Chilli - slit,30 grams Cumin seeds (Jeera)

**Instructions:**
To begin making Kottai Karu Karka Kattal (mutton keema korma), grind all the above mentioned items including coriander powder,tamarind paste, cumin seeds red chillies into fine smooth mixture using some water.
Keep it aside.Heat oil in heavy bottomed pan; add onions saute till they become golden brown.Add garlic pods and fry them well.Now add green chilies and mint leave along with little salt.Stir until everything comes together and cook on low flame.After that add mince meat and mix properly.Cook covered by covering lid so that moisture will evaporate fast and get cooked.Serve hot.Karvu karra katchiyathu goes best when served over rice .

user_message: Generate a Vegetarian North Indian Recipes lunch recipe for Kaju Butter Masala Recipe
reference_text:
**Kaju Butter Masala Recipe**


**Ingredients:**
1 teaspoon Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder,1 teaspoon Garam masala powder,1 cup Whole cashews,Salt - to taste,1 tablespoon Butter,1 Green Chilli - slit,1 tablespoon Ginger Garlic Paste,1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1 teaspoon Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves) - crushed for garnish,4 Tomatoes,3 tablespoons Fresh cream

**Instructions:**
To begin making Kaju Butter Masala Recipe, wash and blanch the tomatoes in enough water, once cool, remove the skin and make a smooth paste along with 8 cashews using a mixer grinder.
Keep aside.Heat butter in a heavy bottomed pan, add the whole cashews and fry until they turn golden and remove them into a plate.
Keep aside until further use.To the same pan add the bay leaf, ginger garlic paste and saute for a minute.
Add the tomato cashew puree, green chilli and cook for 2 minutes.Add the red chilli powder, 2 cups water, season with salt and let it simmer for 5 minutes until the gravy becomes thick and comes together.Add the fried cashews, garam masala powder and simmer for 5 minutes so that the cashews become a little soft.Add the cream and mix well.
Simmer for another 2 minutes and switch off the heat.Slightly crush the kasuri methi between your palms and add it as a garnish over the prepared Kaju Butter Masala.Serve the Kaju Butter Masala Recipe, along with Butter Naan, Tandoori Aloo, Salad for a delicious Weekend Lunch.

user_message: Generate a High Protein Vegetarian Bengali Recipes lunch recipe for Bengali Style Cholar Dal Recipe-Chana Dal
reference_text:
**Bengali Style Cholar Dal Recipe-Chana Dal**


**Ingredients:**
2 Green Chillies,1/2 teaspoon Turmeric powder (Haldi),2 tablespoons Fresh coconut - grated,1 teaspoon Ginger - paste,1 inch Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),2 to 3 Dry Red Chilli,1/2 cup Fresh coconut - slices,3 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds,5 to 6 Raisins,Salt - (adjustable),2 tablespoons Ghee,2 cups Chana dal (Bengal Gram Dal),1/2 teaspoon Sugar,1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),1 pinch Asafoetida (hing)

**Instructions:**
To begin making the Cholar Dal recipe, wash dal under running water nicely till clear water appear.In a pressure cooker, add dal with salt, turmeric, bay leaf, cardamom, cinnamon and water till the dal is immersed.Pressure cook for 2 to 3 whistles as we don't want the dal to get mashed completely.Once the dal is cooked, keep aside and prepare the tempering.Heat ghee in a wok/kadhai and add cumin seeds and asafoetida, wait till cumin crackles.Now add green chilies, ginger paste, raisins and saute for a while.Add in the cooked dal and grated coconut and stir well.Check salt again and adjust if required, cover the lid and cook for 10 to 12 minutes in low flame.Once the dal is done, add a generous pinch of sugar along with some coconut slices before turning off the gas.Serve hot and delicious Cholar Dal with fluffy Bengali Luchi for a perfect Bengali meal.

user_message: Generate a High Protein Vegetarian Himachal side dish recipe for Sepu Vadi Recipe (Himachali Split Urad Dal Dumplings In Spinach Gravy)
reference_text:
**Sepu Vadi Recipe (Himachali Split Urad Dal Dumplings In Spinach Gravy)**


**Ingredients:**
1/4 teaspoon Fennel Powder,1 Green Chilli - finely chopped,1 teaspoon Cumin powder (Jeera),200 grams Spinach,1 teaspoon Coriander Powder (Dhania),1/4 teaspoon Lemon juice,Salt - to taste,1 teaspoon Dill leaves,1/4 teaspoon Sunflower Oil - ¼ and more for frying vadis,1/2 teaspoon Cumin seeds (Jeera),2 Cardamom (Elaichi) Pods/Seeds - pounded,1 cup Curd (Dahi / Yogurt),1/4 teaspoon Black pepper powder - freshly crushed,1 Dry Red Chilli - deseeded,2 Cloves (Laung),1 pinch Enos fruit salt - or soda,1/2 teaspoon Red Chilli powder,1 pinch Asafoetida (hing),1-2 Green Chillies - chopped,1/2 cup Black Urad Dal (Split) - soaked overnight,1 inch Ginger,1 Cinnamon Stick (Dalchini),1 Bay leaf (tej patta),1 tablespoon Mustard oil

**Instructions:**
For the Dumplings (Vadi)To make Sepu Vadi Recipe (Himachali Split Urad Dal Dumplings In Spinach Gravy), make the dumplings by washing and soaking urad dal (split black gram) in about 1.5-2 cups water overnight.Drain and wash the dal.Heat water in a steamer and let it boil.In a blender/grinder jar, combine washed and drained urad dal, ginger, green chilli and grind to make a smooth paste adding about ¼ cup water gradually, so that the mixture is very thick and forms a paste.Take the paste in a bowl and add salt.
Whisk it well till it becomes light and fluffy.Add eno and lemon juice and immediately transfer the mixture to a greased plate and steam for about 15-20 minutes.Remove from steamer and insert a toothpick to check whether it is done.
If toothpick comes out clean, our vadi is ready.
If it doesn’t come out clean, steam for another few minutes more.Meanwhile heat enough oil in a deep frying pan to fry the vadis.Cut the steamed vadis into square pieces.Deep fry the vadi pieces in hot oil till they are crisp and golden in colour.For the Palak PureeSoak Palak leaves in a bowl of water and pinch of salt for about 5 minutes.Remove the stems from the leaves.
Roughly chop the leaves.Add water in a wide pan and bring to a boil.
Then add spinach leaves and cook for 2 minutes.Immediately remove and soak the leaves in a bowl of ice cold water for a minute.
Drain all the excess water.In a mixer/blender jar, add blanched palak leaves, dill leaves and about 1-2 tablespoons water and blend till you get a smooth puree.Keep the spinach puree aside.For the GravyHeat oil in a kadai.
Add dry red chilli, cloves, cinnamon, cardamom, bay leaf and cumin seeds.Once they splutter, add green chilli and hing.
Saute for a couple of seconds.Now turn off the flame and add jeera powder, dhaniya powder, red chili powder, fennel powder and black pepper powder.
Mix well.
Turn on the flame and saute for 1-2 minutes on a low flame so that spice powders does not burn.Add spinach puree and cook for about 2-3 minutes.Now add salt, whisked curd and the fried sepu vadis and let it simmer for 5-6 minutes.
At this stage, you may add required quantity of water as per the consistency of the gravy you prefer.
Switch off heat once the gravy is well combined.Serve Sepu Vadi Recipe (Split Urad Dal Dumplings In Spinach Gravy) with Papad Parathas Recipe, Pudina Tawa Paratha (Mint Leaf Skillet Flat Bread) or steamed rice.
